```gherkin
  Scenario: RTL-Navigation in Menüs, Listen und Paginierung
    # Prüft die korrekte Reihenfolge der UI-Elemente bei Navigation in RTL
    Given eine RTL-Sprache ist aktiv
    When ich durch Menüs, Listen und Paginierung navigiere
    Then befinden sich Navigations- und UI-Elemente in der erwarteten RTL-Reihenfolge
    And vorwärts und rückwärts Navigation folgen der RTL-Ausrichtung

  @regression @happy-path
  Scenario: RTL-Formulareingaben werden rechtsbündig verarbeitet
    # Validiert die korrekte Ausrichtung von Eingabefeldern, Cursor und Platzhaltern sowie das Speichern der Eingabe
    Given eine RTL-Sprache ist aktiv
    When ich ein Formular mit mehreren Feldern ausfülle und absende
    Then sind Eingabefelder rechtsbündig, Cursor und Platzhalter RTL-konform
    And die Eingabe wird korrekt gespeichert und angezeigt

  @regression @edge-case
  Scenario: Gemischte RTL- und LTR-Inhalte bleiben lesbar
    # Überprüft die korrekte Einbettung von LTR-Inhalten in RTL-Kontext
    Given die Anwendung enthält gemischten RTL- und LTR-Text
    When ich Inhalte mit Zahlen, URLs oder LTR-Begriffen ansehe
    Then werden LTR-Inhalte korrekt eingebettet und bleiben lesbar
    And es treten keine Layoutbrüche oder Überlappungen auf

  @regression @boundary
  Scenario: RTL-Formularfelder reagieren korrekt bei leeren Eingaben
    # Boundary-Test für leere Eingaben und Platzhalterausrichtung in RTL-Formularen
    Given eine RTL-Sprache ist aktiv und ein Formular ist geöffnet
    When ich das Formular ohne Eingaben absende
    Then bleiben Platzhalter und Fehlermeldungen rechtsbündig und RTL-konform
    And die Fehlermeldungen beeinträchtigen nicht die Ausrichtung des Layouts

  @negative @regression
  Scenario: RTL-Unterstützung bei nicht unterstützter Sprache
    # Error-Szenario bei einer nicht verfügbaren Spracheinstellung
    Given eine nicht unterstützte Sprache wird ausgewählt
    When ich die Anwendung öffne
    Then wird eine verständliche Fehlermeldung angezeigt
    And die Anwendung fällt auf die zuletzt gültige Spracheinstellung zurück

  @regression @boundary
  Scenario: RTL-Layout bleibt korrekt bei sehr langen RTL-Texten
    # Boundary-Test für lange Texte in RTL-Layouts ohne Überlauf
    Given eine RTL-Sprache ist aktiv und eine Seite mit langen Textblöcken ist verfügbar
    When ich die Seite mit sehr langen RTL-Absätzen öffne
    Then bleiben Textausrichtung und Umbrüche RTL-konform
    And es kommt zu keinem Überlauf außerhalb des Containers

  @regression @edge-case
  Scenario Outline: Datengetriebene Prüfung von gemischten RTL/LTR-Inhalten
    # Scenario Outline zur Prüfung unterschiedlicher LTR-Einbettungen in RTL-Kontext
    Given eine RTL-Sprache ist aktiv und der Inhaltstyp <content_type> wird angezeigt
    When ich den Inhalt betrachte
    Then bleibt der LTR-Abschnitt <ltr_sample> lesbar und korrekt eingebettet
    And das Layout bleibt stabil ohne Verschiebungen

    Examples:
      | content_type | ltr_sample |
      | Zahlen und Datum | 2024-12-31 |
      | URL | https://example.com/path |
      | Technischer Begriff | API v2.1 |

```

### Regionale Formate

*User Story:* US-125

```gherkin
@smoke @regression
Feature: Regionale Formate
  As a end user
  I want to view and enter dates, numbers, and currencies in their local regional format
  So that so that information is understood and processed correctly without confusion or errors

  Background:
    Given the user is authenticated and on a form with date, number, and currency fields

  @happy-path @smoke @regression
  Scenario: Display values in de-DE locale
    # Verify date, number, and currency are displayed in German format
    Given the user locale is set to de-DE
    When the user views the record with date 2024-12-31, number 1234.56, and currency 1234.56 EUR
    Then the system displays the date as 31.12.2024
    And the system displays the number as 1.234,56
    And the system displays the currency as 1.234,56 €

  @happy-path @regression
  Scenario: Accept and store inputs for en-US locale
    # Verify the system accepts and stores input in US format
    Given the user locale is set to en-US
    When the user enters date 12/31/2024 and number 1,234.56
    And the user submits the form
    Then the system stores the date as 2024-12-31
    And the system stores the number as 1234.56

  @negative @regression
  Scenario: Reject input that does not match active locale format
    # Validate error is shown for mismatched regional format
    Given the user locale is set to de-DE
    When the user enters date 12/31/2024 and number 1,234.56
    And the user submits the form
    Then the system shows a validation error indicating the required regional format

  @edge-case @regression
  Scenario Outline: Accept boundary numeric values per locale
    # Verify minimum and maximum numeric values are accepted in locale format
    Given the user locale is set to <locale>
    When the user enters number <input_number>
    And the user submits the form
    Then the system stores the number as <stored_number>

    Examples:
      | locale | input_number | stored_number |
      | en-US | 0.00 | 0.00 |
      | de-DE | 0,01 | 0.01 |
      | en-US | 9,999,999.99 | 9999999.99 |
      | de-DE | 9.999.999,99 | 9999999.99 |

  @edge-case @negative @regression
  Scenario: Handle ambiguous date formats on locale switch
    # Ensure dates are validated against the active locale after switching
    Given the user locale is switched from en-US to de-DE
    When the user enters date 04/05/2024
    And the user submits the form
    Then the system shows a validation error indicating the required regional format

  @edge-case @regression
  Scenario Outline: Validate currency formatting for zero and negative values
    # Verify currency display for boundary and negative values
    Given the user locale is set to <locale>
    When the user views a record with currency value <currency_value>
    Then the system displays the currency as <display_value>

    Examples:
      | locale | currency_value | display_value |
      | en-US | 0 | $0.00 |
      | de-DE | 0 | 0,00 € |
      | en-US | -1234.56 | -$1,234.56 |
      | de-DE | -1234.56 | -1.234,56 € |

```

### Lokale Sticker

*User Story:* US-126

```gherkin
@smoke @regression
Feature: Lokale Sticker
  As a registered customer
  I want to browse and select region-specific sticker packs
  So that so that I can personalize my messages with locally relevant content

  Background:
    Given the customer is registered and logged in
    And the sticker store is available

  @happy-path @smoke @regression
  Scenario Outline: Display region-specific sticker packs when a region is selected
    # Happy path for selecting a region and viewing its sticker packs
    Given the sticker store shows a list of available regions
    When the customer selects the region "<region>"
    Then the system displays sticker packs specific to "<region>"
    And no sticker packs from other regions are shown

    Examples:
      | region |
      | Bavaria |
      | California |

  @happy-path @regression
  Scenario Outline: Pre-filter sticker packs based on customer's profile region
    # Happy path for highlighting or pre-filtering by profile region
    Given the customer has "<profile_region>" set in their profile
    When the customer opens the sticker store
    Then sticker packs for "<profile_region>" are highlighted or pre-filtered
    And the selected region filter shows "<profile_region>"

    Examples:
      | profile_region |
      | Tokyo |
      | Ontario |

  @edge-case @regression
  Scenario Outline: No sticker packs available for selected region
    # Edge case when the selected region has no packs
    Given the region "<region>" has no sticker packs
    When the customer selects the region "<region>"
    Then the system shows a message indicating no regional packs are available
    And the system offers an option to view all packs

    Examples:
      | region |
      | Antarctica |

  @negative @regression
  Scenario Outline: Handle invalid region selection
    # Error scenario for selecting a region not in the available list
    Given the available regions list does not include "<invalid_region>"
    When the customer attempts to select "<invalid_region>"
    Then the system prevents the selection and shows an error message
    And the region filter remains unchanged

    Examples:
      | invalid_region |
      | Atlantis |

  @boundary @regression
  Scenario Outline: Boundary condition for regions list size
    # Boundary test for minimum and maximum number of available regions
    Given the sticker store has "<region_count>" available regions
    When the customer opens the region selector
    Then all "<region_count>" regions are displayed without truncation
    And the customer can select any listed region

    Examples:
      | region_count |
      | 1 |
      | 50 |

```

---

## Test Cases

### TC-001: Successful registration with valid phone and correct code

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Verify account creation and phone verification with valid unused number and correct code within time limit

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen
- Phone number is valid and unused
- SMS delivery service is operational

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a valid unused phone number in the phone field | Phone number is accepted and no validation error is shown |
| 2 | 2. Submit the registration form | Verification code is sent and code entry screen is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter the correct verification code within the allowed time | Code is accepted and verification succeeds |

**Final Expected Result:** Account is created and phone number is marked as verified

---

### TC-002: Reject invalid phone number format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Ensure improperly formatted phone numbers are rejected with validation message

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an invalid phone number format (e.g., '123-ABC') | Validation error is displayed for phone number field |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit the registration form | Form submission is blocked and validation message remains visible |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects invalid phone input and displays a validation message

---

### TC-003: Reject empty phone number field

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Ensure empty phone number input cannot be submitted

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave the phone number field empty | Field is marked as required or shows no entry |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit the registration form | Submission is blocked and a required-field validation message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents submission and informs user that phone number is required

---

### TC-004: Boundary test: minimum valid phone number length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Verify system accepts phone number at the minimum valid length

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen
- Minimum length rule is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a phone number with the minimum valid length | Phone number is accepted without validation error |
| 2 | 2. Submit the registration form | Verification code is sent and code entry screen is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** System accepts minimum-length valid phone number and initiates verification

---

### TC-005: Boundary test: phone number exceeds maximum length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Verify system rejects phone number exceeding maximum allowed length

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen
- Maximum length rule is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a phone number longer than the maximum allowed length | Validation error is displayed for phone number field |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit the registration form | Submission is blocked and validation error persists |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects phone numbers exceeding max length

---

### TC-006: Incorrect verification code

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Ensure incorrect verification code is denied and user can request a new code

**Preconditions:**
- User has submitted a valid phone number
- Verification code has been sent

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an incorrect verification code | System shows error indicating code is incorrect |
| 2 | 2. Select the option to request a new code | System allows request and sends a new verification code |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification is denied for incorrect code and user can request a new code

---

### TC-007: Expired verification code

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Ensure expired code is denied and user can request a new code

**Preconditions:**
- User has submitted a valid phone number
- Verification code has been sent
- Code expiry time has passed

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter the expired verification code | System indicates code is expired |
| 2 | 2. Select the option to request a new code | System allows request and sends a new verification code |

**Final Expected Result:** Expired code is rejected and user can request a new code

---

### TC-008: Registration with already used phone number

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Ensure system prevents registration with a phone number already registered

**Preconditions:**
- User is on registration screen
- Phone number is already registered in the system

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a phone number that is already registered | System recognizes number and shows a validation or error message |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit the registration form | Submission is blocked and user is informed the number is already in use |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects registration with an already used phone number

---

### TC-009: Resend verification code and verify with new code

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-001
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-001

**Description:** Verify user can request a new code and complete verification with the latest code

**Preconditions:**
- User has submitted a valid phone number
- Initial verification code has been sent

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Resend code' option | System sends a new verification code and indicates it has been sent |
| 2 | 2. Enter the newly received verification code | System accepts the new code |

**Final Expected Result:** User verifies successfully using the resent code and account is created

---

### TC-010: Enable 2FA with valid 6-digit PIN

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify user can enable 2FA by providing a valid 6-digit PIN and it is activated for future logins

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to security settings
- 2FA is currently disabled for the user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Security Settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Enable 2FA' | PIN verification prompt is shown |
| 3 | 3. Enter a valid 6-digit PIN and submit | 2FA activation is confirmed |

**Final Expected Result:** 2FA is activated and required for subsequent logins

---

### TC-011: Login with 2FA enabled and correct PIN

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify login succeeds when correct credentials and correct 6-digit PIN are provided

**Preconditions:**
- User has 2FA enabled
- User has valid login credentials

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to login page | Login form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid username and password and submit | PIN entry screen is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter the correct 6-digit PIN and submit | User is authenticated |

**Final Expected Result:** User is granted access to the application

---

### TC-012: Login with 2FA enabled and incorrect PIN

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify access is denied when an incorrect 6-digit PIN is entered

**Preconditions:**
- User has 2FA enabled
- User has valid login credentials

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to login page | Login form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid username and password and submit | PIN entry screen is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter an incorrect 6-digit PIN and submit | Error message is shown and access is denied |

**Final Expected Result:** User remains unauthenticated and receives an error message

---

### TC-013: Disable 2FA from security settings

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify user can disable 2FA and it is not required on subsequent logins

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to security settings
- 2FA is currently enabled for the user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Security Settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Disable 2FA' | 2FA deactivation is confirmed |
| 3 | 3. Log out and log back in with valid credentials | User is logged in without being prompted for PIN |

**Final Expected Result:** 2FA is deactivated and no PIN is required for login

---

### TC-014: Enable 2FA with invalid PIN length (5 digits)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify system rejects PINs shorter than 6 digits during 2FA enablement

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to security settings
- 2FA is currently disabled for the user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Security Settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Enable 2FA' | PIN verification prompt is shown |
| 3 | 3. Enter a 5-digit PIN and submit | Validation error indicating PIN must be 6 digits |

**Final Expected Result:** 2FA is not activated and an error message is displayed

---

### TC-015: Enable 2FA with invalid PIN length (7 digits)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify system rejects PINs longer than 6 digits during 2FA enablement

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to security settings
- 2FA is currently disabled for the user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Security Settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Enable 2FA' | PIN verification prompt is shown |
| 3 | 3. Enter a 7-digit PIN and submit | Validation error indicating PIN must be 6 digits |

**Final Expected Result:** 2FA is not activated and an error message is displayed

---

### TC-016: Enable 2FA with non-numeric PIN

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify system rejects non-numeric PIN during 2FA enablement

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to security settings
- 2FA is currently disabled for the user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Security Settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Enable 2FA' | PIN verification prompt is shown |
| 3 | 3. Enter a non-numeric PIN (e.g., 12A45B) and submit | Validation error indicating PIN must be numeric |

**Final Expected Result:** 2FA is not activated and an error message is displayed

---

### TC-017: Login with 2FA enabled and boundary PIN 000000

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify boundary numeric PINs are accepted if correct

**Preconditions:**
- User has 2FA enabled with PIN set to 000000
- User has valid login credentials

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to login page | Login form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid username and password and submit | PIN entry screen is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter PIN 000000 and submit | User is authenticated |

**Final Expected Result:** User is granted access using boundary PIN

---

### TC-018: Login with 2FA disabled after deactivation

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-002
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-002

**Description:** Verify user is not prompted for PIN after disabling 2FA

**Preconditions:**
- User has disabled 2FA
- User has valid login credentials

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to login page | Login form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter valid username and password and submit | User is authenticated without PIN prompt |

**Final Expected Result:** User is logged in successfully without 2FA

---

### TC-019: Biometrische Entsperrung erfolgreich bei aktivierter und eingerichteter Biometrie

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass die App bei aktivierter und eingerichteter Biometrie eine erfolgreiche Authentifizierung per Fingerabdruck/Face ID ermöglicht

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet
- App ist installiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Gültigen Fingerabdruck/Face ID bereitstellen | Biometrische Prüfung wird erfolgreich abgeschlossen |
| 3 | 3. Warten, bis die App weiterleitet | Benutzer erhält Zugriff auf die App (z. B. Startseite) |

**Final Expected Result:** Zugang zur App wird ohne Passworteingabe gewährt

---

### TC-020: Biometrische Entsperrung schlägt fehl und alternative Anmeldung wird angeboten

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass nach fehlgeschlagener Biometrie alternative Anmeldung angeboten wird

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Ungültigen Fingerabdruck/Face ID bereitstellen | Biometrische Prüfung schlägt fehl |
| 3 | 3. Fehlerdialog/Alternative auswählen | Anmeldeoptionen mit Passwort oder PIN werden angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Benutzer kann sich alternativ per Passwort oder PIN anmelden

---

### TC-021: Mehrfaches Fehlschlagen der Biometrie führt zur Alternative

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft Verhalten bei mehreren fehlgeschlagenen Biometrieversuchen (Boundary Condition)

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet
- Gerät unterstützt begrenzte Fehlversuche

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Mehrfach ungültige biometrische Daten bereitstellen (bis zur Gerätegrenze) | Biometrische Prüfung schlägt wiederholt fehl |
| 3 | 3. Warten, bis die App reagiert | Alternative Anmeldung mit Passwort oder PIN wird angeboten |

**Final Expected Result:** Nach mehreren Fehlversuchen wird eine alternative Anmeldung erzwungen

---

### TC-022: Biometrische Option wird nicht angeboten, wenn Gerät keine Biometrie unterstützt

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass die App keine biometrische Option anbietet, wenn das Gerät Biometrie nicht unterstützt

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Gerät unterstützt keine Biometrie
- App ist installiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Kein biometrischer Authentifizierungsdialog erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Anmeldebildschirm prüfen | Nur Passwort/PIN-Anmeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Biometrische Entsperrung wird nicht angeboten

---

### TC-023: Biometrische Option wird nicht angeboten, wenn Biometrie nicht eingerichtet ist

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass keine biometrische Option angezeigt wird, wenn Biometrie auf dem Gerät nicht eingerichtet ist

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Gerät unterstützt Biometrie
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät nicht eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Kein biometrischer Authentifizierungsdialog erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Anmeldebildschirm prüfen | Nur Passwort/PIN-Anmeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Biometrische Entsperrung wird nicht angeboten

---

### TC-024: Biometrische Entsperrung deaktiviert in der App

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass bei deaktivierter biometrischer Entsperrung kein biometrischer Dialog angezeigt wird

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App deaktiviert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Kein biometrischer Authentifizierungsdialog erscheint |
| 2 | 2. Anmeldebildschirm prüfen | Passwort/PIN-Anmeldung wird angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Anmeldung erfolgt ausschließlich über Passwort oder PIN

---

### TC-025: Fallback-Anmeldung mit Passwort nach Biometriefehler erfolgreich

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass der Benutzer sich nach Biometriefehler mit korrektem Passwort anmelden kann

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet
- Benutzer kennt gültiges Passwort

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Ungültige Biometrie bereitstellen | Biometrische Prüfung schlägt fehl |
| 3 | 3. Alternative Anmeldung mit Passwort auswählen | Passworteingabefeld wird angezeigt |
| 4 | 4. Gültiges Passwort eingeben und bestätigen | Anmeldung erfolgreich |

**Final Expected Result:** Benutzer erhält Zugriff auf die App

---

### TC-026: Fallback-Anmeldung mit PIN nach Biometriefehler erfolgreich

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass der Benutzer sich nach Biometriefehler mit korrekter PIN anmelden kann

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet
- Benutzer kennt gültige PIN

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Ungültige Biometrie bereitstellen | Biometrische Prüfung schlägt fehl |
| 3 | 3. Alternative Anmeldung mit PIN auswählen | PIN-Eingabefeld wird angezeigt |
| 4 | 4. Gültige PIN eingeben und bestätigen | Anmeldung erfolgreich |

**Final Expected Result:** Benutzer erhält Zugriff auf die App

---

### TC-027: Abbruch der biometrischen Authentifizierung durch den Benutzer

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-003
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-003

**Description:** Prüft, dass bei Abbruch der Biometrie alternative Anmeldung verfügbar ist

**Preconditions:**
- Benutzer ist registriert
- Biometrische Entsperrung ist in der App aktiviert
- Biometrie ist auf dem Gerät eingerichtet

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. App öffnen | Biometrische Authentifizierungsaufforderung wird angezeigt |
| 2 | 2. Biometrieabfrage abbrechen | Biometrie wird nicht durchgeführt |
| 3 | 3. Prüfen, ob alternative Anmeldung angeboten wird | Passwort/PIN-Optionen werden angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Benutzer kann sich mit Passwort oder PIN anmelden

---

### TC-028: Concurrent login on two devices remains active

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify that sessions on Device A and Device B remain active when logging in with the same account

**Preconditions:**
- Registered user account exists
- Device A and Device B have network access

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device A, navigate to the login page and sign in with valid credentials | User is authenticated on Device A and lands on the dashboard |
| 2 | 2. On Device B, navigate to the login page and sign in with the same valid credentials | User is authenticated on Device B and lands on the dashboard |
| 3 | 3. On Device A, refresh the page or navigate to another protected page | Device A session remains active without re-authentication |

**Final Expected Result:** Both Device A and Device B maintain active, usable sessions concurrently

---

### TC-029: Action on Device A does not terminate Device B session

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify that performing an action on Device A does not log out Device B

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- A writable resource exists (e.g., task or note)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device A, create or update a resource | Action completes successfully and confirmation is shown |
| 2 | 2. On Device B, navigate to a protected page or refresh current page | Device B remains authenticated and is not logged out |

**Final Expected Result:** Action on Device A is processed successfully without terminating Device B session

---

### TC-030: Action on Device B does not terminate Device A session

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify that performing an action on Device B does not log out Device A

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- A writable resource exists (e.g., task or note)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device B, delete or update a resource | Action completes successfully and confirmation is shown |
| 2 | 2. On Device A, navigate to a protected page or refresh current page | Device A remains authenticated and is not logged out |

**Final Expected Result:** Action on Device B is processed successfully without terminating Device A session

---

### TC-031: Third device login is allowed without affecting existing sessions

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify that logging in on a third device does not block or log out other devices

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- Device C has network access

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device C, navigate to login page and sign in with the same valid credentials | User is authenticated on Device C and lands on the dashboard |
| 2 | 2. On Device A, refresh a protected page | Device A remains authenticated |
| 3 | 3. On Device B, refresh a protected page | Device B remains authenticated |

**Final Expected Result:** Device C session is created without blocking or logging out Device A or Device B

---

### TC-032: Simultaneous login attempts from multiple devices

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify system behavior when logging in simultaneously on two devices

**Preconditions:**
- Registered user account exists
- Device A and Device B are ready to submit login at the same time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device A and Device B, enter valid credentials | Credentials are accepted on both devices |
| 2 | 2. Submit login on both devices within the same second | Both devices are authenticated without conflict |

**Final Expected Result:** Concurrent login submissions result in active sessions on both devices

---

### TC-033: Session persistence across devices after inactivity

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify sessions remain active across devices after short inactivity within session timeout window

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- Session timeout is configured (e.g., 30 minutes)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave Device A idle for a duration less than session timeout (e.g., 10 minutes) | No automatic logout occurs on Device A |
| 2 | 2. Perform an action on Device B during Device A's idle period | Action succeeds on Device B |
| 3 | 3. Return to Device A and navigate to a protected page | Device A remains authenticated |

**Final Expected Result:** Sessions on both devices remain active and usable within the timeout window

---

### TC-034: Invalid credentials on Device B do not affect active session on Device A

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Negative test to ensure failed login on Device B does not log out Device A

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A
- Device B is logged out

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device B, attempt to log in with incorrect password for the same account | Login fails with an error message |
| 2 | 2. On Device A, refresh a protected page | Device A remains authenticated and session is intact |

**Final Expected Result:** Failed login on Device B does not impact Device A session

---

### TC-035: Concurrent actions on two devices are processed successfully

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Verify that actions executed concurrently on Device A and Device B both succeed

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- Two independent writable resources exist

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On Device A, update Resource 1 | Resource 1 update succeeds |
| 2 | 2. At the same time on Device B, update Resource 2 | Resource 2 update succeeds |

**Final Expected Result:** Both actions are processed successfully without session termination

---

### TC-036: High number of concurrent device sessions allowed

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Boundary test to validate system allows multiple concurrent sessions without forced logout

**Preconditions:**
- Registered user account exists
- Multiple devices or browser instances are available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log in with the same account on 5 different devices or browser instances | All 5 sessions are authenticated successfully |
| 2 | 2. Refresh each session and perform a simple action (e.g., view dashboard) | Each session remains active and actions succeed |

**Final Expected Result:** System supports multiple concurrent sessions without forced logout

---

### TC-037: Session revocation not triggered by concurrent usage

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-004
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-004

**Description:** Negative test to ensure system does not revoke sessions when multiple devices are used

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A and Device B
- Audit/logging is enabled for session events

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Perform a login on Device C using the same account | Device C is authenticated successfully |
| 2 | 2. Check session status on Device A and Device B | Both sessions remain active and show no forced logout message |

**Final Expected Result:** Concurrent usage does not trigger session revocation on any device

---

### TC-038: Successful passkey login with registered passkey

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify user can log in using a previously registered passkey and complete device authentication successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has at least one registered passkey
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | Passkey prompt is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Complete device authentication (e.g., biometrics/OS prompt) | Authentication succeeds and user is logged in |

**Final Expected Result:** User is authenticated and redirected to their account/dashboard

---

### TC-039: Passkey login selected with no registered passkey

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify system informs user that no passkey exists and offers alternative login

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has no registered passkeys

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | System checks for registered passkeys |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | User is informed no passkey is available and alternative login options are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** User is not logged in and can choose an alternative login method

---

### TC-040: Passkey authentication cancelled by user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify login is denied and user can retry or choose another method when device auth is cancelled

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has at least one registered passkey
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | Passkey prompt is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Cancel the device authentication prompt | Authentication does not complete |
| 4 | 4. Observe the login state and options | Login is denied and retry/alternative methods are offered |

**Final Expected Result:** User remains logged out and can retry passkey login or select another login method

---

### TC-041: Passkey authentication fails (biometric mismatch)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify login is denied when device authentication fails and user can retry or choose another method

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has at least one registered passkey
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | Passkey prompt is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Fail device authentication (e.g., incorrect biometric) | Authentication fails |
| 4 | 4. Observe the login state and options | Login is denied and retry/alternative methods are offered |

**Final Expected Result:** User remains logged out and can retry passkey login or select another login method

---

### TC-042: Retry passkey login after initial failure

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify user can retry passkey authentication after a failure and log in successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has at least one registered passkey
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | Passkey prompt is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Fail or cancel the device authentication | Authentication does not complete |
| 4 | 4. Select retry for passkey login | Passkey prompt is re-initiated |
| 5 | 5. Complete device authentication successfully | User is logged in |

**Final Expected Result:** User is authenticated and redirected to their account/dashboard

---

### TC-043: Choose alternative login after no passkey available

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify user can switch to an alternative login method when no passkey is registered

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has no registered passkeys
- Alternative login method is enabled (e.g., password, OTP)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | System indicates no passkey is available and shows alternatives |
| 3 | 3. Select the alternative login method | Alternative login form is displayed |
| 4 | 4. Complete alternative login with valid credentials | User is logged in |

**Final Expected Result:** User is authenticated using the alternative method

---

### TC-044: Multiple registered passkeys selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify system handles multiple passkeys and allows successful login with one

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has multiple registered passkeys
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | Passkey selection/OS prompt is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Choose one of the available passkeys and complete authentication | Authentication succeeds and user is logged in |

**Final Expected Result:** User is authenticated and logged in using the selected passkey

---

### TC-045: Device does not support passkey authentication

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify system handles unsupported device and offers alternative login method

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has a registered passkey
- Device does not support passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page on an unsupported device | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option | System detects unsupported device or fails to initiate passkey flow |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | User is informed that passkey login is not available and alternatives are offered |

**Final Expected Result:** User is not logged in and can choose an alternative login method

---

### TC-046: Passkey login response time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-005
**Requirement:** WA-AUTH-005

**Description:** Verify passkey login completes within acceptable time under normal conditions

**Preconditions:**
- User has an existing account
- User has at least one registered passkey
- Device supports passkey authentication

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the login page | Login options are displayed including passkey login |
| 2 | 2. Select the passkey login option and complete device authentication | Authentication completes within the defined performance threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** User is logged in and total passkey login time meets performance requirements

---

### TC-047: Upload valid profile picture within size limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that a valid image upload saves and displays as current profile picture

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- Valid image file available within size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid image file within the allowed size limit and confirm | Upload starts and completes successfully |
| 3 | 3. Observe the profile picture area | Newly uploaded image is displayed as the current profile picture |

**Final Expected Result:** System saves the image and displays it as the current profile picture

---

### TC-048: Replace existing profile picture with a new valid image

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that uploading a new valid image replaces the existing profile picture

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- User already has a profile picture
- Valid image file available within size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a different valid image file within the allowed size limit and confirm | Upload completes successfully |
| 3 | 3. Refresh the profile settings page | The newly uploaded image persists as the current profile picture |

**Final Expected Result:** Existing profile picture is replaced by the new image

---

### TC-049: Reject non-image file upload

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that non-image files are rejected with a clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- Non-image file available (e.g., .pdf or .txt)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select a non-image file and confirm | Upload is rejected |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI message area | A clear error message indicates only image files are allowed |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects non-image upload and displays a clear error message

---

### TC-050: Reject image file exceeding size limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that images exceeding the size limit are rejected with a clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- Image file available that exceeds size limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select an image file that exceeds the size limit and confirm | Upload is rejected |
| 3 | 3. Observe the UI message area | A clear error message indicates file size exceeds the limit |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects oversized image upload and displays a clear error message

---

### TC-051: Boundary test: upload image at exact size limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that an image equal to the maximum allowed size is accepted

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- Image file exactly at size limit available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select the image file that is exactly at the size limit and confirm | Upload completes successfully |
| 3 | 3. Observe the profile picture area | Uploaded image is displayed as the current profile picture |

**Final Expected Result:** System accepts and displays the image at the size limit

---

### TC-052: Remove existing profile picture

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that removing a profile picture deletes it and shows default placeholder

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- User already has a profile picture

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the remove/delete profile picture action | Removal confirmation (if applicable) is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Confirm removal | Profile picture is removed |
| 3 | 3. Observe the profile picture area | Default placeholder image is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Profile picture is deleted and default placeholder is shown

---

### TC-053: Persistence of profile picture across sessions

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that uploaded profile picture persists after logout and login

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has a profile picture uploaded

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out from the application | User is logged out successfully |
| 2 | 2. Log in again with the same account | User is logged in successfully |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile settings page | Profile settings page loads |
| 4 | 4. Observe the profile picture area | Previously uploaded profile picture is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Profile picture persists across sessions

---

### TC-054: Cancel upload action

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-006
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-001

**Description:** Verify that canceling file selection does not change the current profile picture

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile settings page
- User may or may not have a profile picture

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click on the profile picture upload button | File picker dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Cancel the file picker dialog without selecting a file | Dialog closes without upload |
| 3 | 3. Observe the profile picture area | Profile picture remains unchanged |

**Final Expected Result:** No changes are made when upload is canceled

---

### TC-055: Save valid display name and verify shown across app

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify a valid display name can be saved and is displayed wherever the user's name appears

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page
- User has an existing account with a default name

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a valid display name (e.g., 'Alex Morgan') in the display name field | The display name field contains the entered value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A success confirmation is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to the user profile page | The profile shows 'Alex Morgan' as the user's name |
| 4 | 4. Navigate to a page where the user's name is displayed (e.g., header/avatar menu) | The display name 'Alex Morgan' is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** The system stores the display name and shows it wherever the user's name is displayed

---

### TC-056: Default name shown when no display name is set

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify the default account name is shown when no display name is configured

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has never set a display name or it is empty
- User is on the profile page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the user profile page | Profile page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Observe the name displayed on the profile | The default account name is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** The system shows the default name configured for the account

---

### TC-057: Reject empty display name

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify an empty display name is rejected with a validation message and not saved

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Clear the display name field so it is empty | The display name field is empty |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A validation message indicates the display name is required |
| 3 | 3. Reload or revisit the profile settings page | The display name remains unchanged from its previous value |

**Final Expected Result:** The system rejects the empty value, shows a validation message, and does not save changes

---

### TC-058: Reject invalid display name with special characters only

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify an invalid display name is rejected and not saved

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter an invalid display name consisting of special characters only (e.g., '!!!@@@') | The display name field contains the entered value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A validation message indicates the display name is invalid |
| 3 | 3. Navigate away and return to the profile settings page | The invalid display name is not saved |

**Final Expected Result:** The system rejects the invalid display name and does not save it

---

### TC-059: Boundary test: minimum length display name

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify the minimum allowed length display name is accepted and saved

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page
- Minimum display name length is defined (e.g., 2 characters)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a display name at the minimum length (e.g., 'Al') | The display name field contains the entered value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A success confirmation is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Open the profile page | The profile shows the minimum-length display name |

**Final Expected Result:** The system accepts and stores the minimum length display name

---

### TC-060: Boundary test: maximum length display name

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify the maximum allowed length display name is accepted and saved

- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page
- Maximum display name length is defined (e.g., 50 characters)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a display name at the maximum length | The display name field contains the entered maximum-length value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A success confirmation is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to the profile page | The maximum-length display name is displayed correctly without truncation |

**Final Expected Result:** The system accepts and stores the maximum length display name

---

### TC-061: Reject display name exceeding maximum length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify a display name longer than the maximum length is rejected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page
- Maximum display name length is defined (e.g., 50 characters)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a display name that exceeds the maximum length | The display name field contains the over-limit value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A validation message indicates the display name is too long |
| 3 | 3. Refresh the page | The over-limit display name is not saved |

**Final Expected Result:** The system rejects an over-maximum display name and does not save it

---

### TC-062: Update existing display name

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify a previously saved display name can be updated

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing display name saved
- User is on the profile settings page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Replace the existing display name with a new valid value (e.g., 'Taylor Lee') | The display name field contains the new value |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A success confirmation is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Open the profile page | The new display name 'Taylor Lee' is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** The system updates and displays the new display name

---

### TC-063: Persistence after logout/login

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify display name persists across sessions

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has saved a valid display name

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out of the application | User is logged out successfully |
| 2 | 2. Log back in with the same account | Login is successful and user is authenticated |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to the profile page | The saved display name is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** The saved display name persists across sessions

---

### TC-064: Whitespace-only display name rejected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-007
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-002

**Description:** Verify a display name containing only whitespace is rejected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on the profile settings page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a display name with only spaces (e.g., '   ') | The display name field contains the whitespace |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | A validation message indicates the display name is invalid |
| 3 | 3. Reload the profile settings page | The whitespace-only value is not saved |

**Final Expected Result:** The system rejects whitespace-only display names and does not save them

---

### TC-065: Save valid info/status text within allowed length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that a valid status text is saved and displayed

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Maximum status length is known (e.g., 160 characters)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a status text within the allowed length in the info/status field | The text is accepted in the input field |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile view page | The saved status text is displayed in the profile view |

**Final Expected Result:** Valid status text is persisted and shown on the profile view

---

### TC-066: Save empty info/status text

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that empty status text saves without error and no text is shown

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Clear the info/status text field so it is empty | The field is empty with no validation errors |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile view page | No status text is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Profile saves successfully with no status text shown

---

### TC-067: Prevent saving when status text exceeds maximum length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify validation when status text exceeds maximum length

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Maximum status length is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a status text that exceeds the maximum allowed length by 1 character | The field accepts input but is over the limit |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save is prevented and a validation message is displayed indicating the length limit |
| 3 | 3. Refresh or navigate to profile view page | The status text is not updated |

**Final Expected Result:** System blocks save and shows length validation error; no update occurs

---

### TC-068: Save status text at exact maximum length boundary

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that status text at exact maximum length saves successfully

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Maximum status length is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a status text with exactly the maximum allowed characters | The input is accepted without validation errors |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile view page | The status text is displayed correctly |

**Final Expected Result:** Status text at maximum length is saved and displayed

---

### TC-069: Update existing status text with a new valid value

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that an existing status text can be updated

- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Existing status text is present

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Replace the existing status text with a new valid text within length | New text appears in the input field |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile view page | The new status text is displayed and the old text is not shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Existing status is successfully updated

---

### TC-070: Clear existing status text by saving empty value

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that an existing status text can be removed by saving empty

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Existing status text is present

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Delete all text from the info/status field | The field becomes empty |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to profile view page | No status text is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Existing status text is removed successfully

---

### TC-071: Validation message appears only when exceeding maximum length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify validation message behavior for over-limit input

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Maximum status length is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a status text within the allowed length | No validation message is shown |
| 2 | 2. Append characters to exceed maximum length | Validation message appears indicating length limit |
| 3 | 3. Remove characters to return within the limit | Validation message disappears |

**Final Expected Result:** Validation message triggers only for over-limit input and clears when corrected

---

### TC-072: Persist status text after page reload

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that saved status text persists after reload

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter a valid status text within the allowed length | The input field shows the entered text |
| 2 | 2. Click the Save button | Save operation completes without errors |
| 3 | 3. Reload the profile view page | The saved status text is still displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Saved status persists across page reloads

---

### TC-073: Profile save does not occur when over-limit text is entered

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-008
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-003

**Description:** Verify that no partial profile updates occur on validation failure

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on profile edit page
- Other profile fields have values that can be changed

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Modify another profile field (e.g., display name) | The other field shows the new value |
| 2 | 2. Enter an over-limit status text | Validation message appears for the status field |
| 3 | 3. Click the Save button | Save is blocked and validation message remains |
| 4 | 4. Navigate to profile view page | Neither the over-limit status nor the other field changes are saved |

**Final Expected Result:** Profile save is fully blocked when status text is invalid

### TC-074: Display saved phone number on profile

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-009
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-004

**Description:** Verify that a saved phone number is displayed for a logged-in user

- User account exists
- User is logged in
- User has a phone number saved in profile

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the profile page | Profile page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Locate the phone number field | Phone number field is visible |
| 3 | 3. Read the value displayed in the phone number field | The saved phone number is displayed exactly as stored |

**Final Expected Result:** The user's saved phone number is shown in the designated phone number field

---

### TC-075: Show empty phone field when no phone number is saved

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-009
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-004

**Description:** Verify that the phone number field is empty or shows a prompt when no number is saved

**Preconditions:**
- User account exists
- User is logged in
- User has no phone number saved in profile

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the profile page | Profile page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Locate the phone number field | Phone number field is visible |
| 3 | 3. Check the phone number field value or prompt text | Field is empty or shows a prompt to add a phone number |

**Final Expected Result:** No phone number is displayed and an empty value or prompt is shown

### TC-076: Redirect unauthenticated user to login when accessing profile URL

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-009
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-004

**Description:** Ensure unauthenticated users cannot view the phone number on profile page

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged out
- Profile page URL is known

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter the profile page URL directly in the browser | Request is made to access the profile page |
| 2 | 2. Observe the navigation outcome | User is redirected to the login page |
| 3 | 3. Check the page content for phone number | Phone number is not displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthenticated users are redirected to login and cannot view phone number

### TC-077: Phone number formatting is displayed as stored

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-009

| 2 | 2. Locate the phone number field | Phone number field is visible |
| 3 | 3. Compare the displayed phone number with the stored value | Displayed value matches stored format |

**Final Expected Result:** The phone number is displayed exactly as stored without unintended formatting changes

### TC-080: Profile page displays phone number after session refresh

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-009
**Requirement:** WA-PROF-004

**Description:** Ensure phone number is still displayed correctly after page refresh

- User has a phone number saved in profile

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the profile page | Profile page loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Refresh the browser page | Profile page reloads successfully |

| 4 | 4. Verify the displayed phone number | Saved phone number is still displayed correctly |
