### TC-310: Establish encrypted voice call with available party

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that an authenticated user can establish an end-to-end encrypted call when the other party is available

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is authenticated and available
- Both users support encrypted calls
- Network conditions are normal

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A opens the contact list and selects User B | User B contact details are displayed with call options |
| 2 | 2. User A taps the voice call button | Call initiation screen appears and ringing starts for User B |
| 3 | 3. User B accepts the call | Call connects successfully for both users |
| 4 | 4. User A checks the call security indicator | Indicator shows end-to-end encryption is enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Voice call is established with end-to-end encryption enabled

---

### TC-311: Encrypted call initiation when user is not authenticated

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that call initiation is blocked for unauthenticated users

**Preconditions:**
- User A is not authenticated
- User B is available and supports encrypted calls

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A attempts to open the voice call feature | User is prompted to log in or access is denied |
| 2 | 2. User A attempts to initiate a voice call to User B | Call is not initiated |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthenticated user cannot initiate an encrypted voice call

---

### TC-312: Call initiation with other party unavailable

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify call cannot be established when the other party is unavailable

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is unavailable (offline or busy)
- Both users support encrypted calls

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A selects User B and taps the voice call button | System attempts to initiate the call |
| 2 | 2. System evaluates User B availability | User B is shown as unavailable |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is not established and User A receives an unavailable notification

---

### TC-313: Maintain encryption under low-bandwidth on call start

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that encryption is maintained and adaptive quality is applied on low bandwidth at call start

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is authenticated and available
- Both users support encrypted calls
- Network is throttled to low bandwidth before call start

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a voice call to User B under low bandwidth | Call initiation proceeds with possible delay |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connects with adaptive audio quality |
| 3 | 3. User A checks the encryption indicator | Indicator shows end-to-end encryption is enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Call connects with encryption maintained and adaptive quality applied

---

### TC-314: Low-bandwidth during ongoing call with quality notification

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that encryption is maintained and user is notified when quality degrades mid-call

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B are authenticated and in an active encrypted call
- Both users support encrypted calls

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Reduce network bandwidth to low during the active call | Call quality degrades but remains connected |
| 2 | 2. Observe system notifications | User receives a clear notification about reduced call quality |
| 3 | 3. Check the encryption indicator | Indicator still shows end-to-end encryption is enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Ongoing call maintains encryption and notifies user of quality impact

---

### TC-315: Fail call when other party does not support encryption

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that call is not established and user is informed if the other party lacks encrypted call support

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is available but does not support encrypted calls

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a voice call to User B | System attempts to negotiate encryption |
| 2 | 2. Encryption negotiation fails due to unsupported capability | Call setup stops |
| 3 | 3. User A views the error message | User is informed that encryption is not supported by the other party |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is not established and encryption failure message is shown

---

### TC-316: Fail call when encryption setup fails

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify that call is not established when encryption setup fails for technical reasons

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is authenticated and available
- Both users support encrypted calls
- Encryption service is misconfigured or unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a voice call to User B | System attempts to establish encryption session |
| 2 | 2. Encryption setup fails due to service error | Call does not connect |
| 3 | 3. User A checks the error message | User is informed that encryption setup failed |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is not established and user is notified of encryption failure

---

### TC-317: Boundary: minimal acceptable bandwidth maintains encrypted call

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Verify call remains encrypted and connected at the minimum acceptable bandwidth threshold

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B are authenticated and available
- Both users support encrypted calls
- Network bandwidth is set to the minimum supported threshold

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a voice call to User B at minimum bandwidth | Call initiation proceeds with possible delay |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connects with reduced but acceptable quality |
| 3 | 3. Verify encryption status | End-to-end encryption indicator is enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Call remains connected and encrypted at minimum acceptable bandwidth

---

### TC-318: Verify call is blocked when encryption cannot be verified

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-035
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-001

**Description:** Ensure call is not established if encryption status cannot be confirmed during setup

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated
- User B is authenticated and available
- Both users support encrypted calls
- Encryption verification endpoint is unreachable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a voice call to User B | System attempts encryption verification |
| 2 | 2. Encryption verification times out | Call setup stops |
| 3 | 3. User A views notification | User is informed that encryption could not be verified |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is not established and user receives a clear encryption verification failure message

---

### TC-319: Encrypted video call starts successfully between two online users

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can start an encrypted video call with another online user

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Callee is logged in and online
- Both users have stable internet connection
- Camera and microphone permissions are granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Caller opens the contact list and selects the online callee | Callee profile/contact is displayed with call options |
| 2 | 2. Caller clicks the 'Start Video Call' button | Call setup begins and ringing/connecting status is shown |
| 3 | 3. Callee accepts the incoming video call | Video call is established and live video/audio starts |
| 4 | 4. Verify call security indicator during the call | Encryption indicator shows active encryption for the entire call |

**Final Expected Result:** Video call is successfully established and remains encrypted throughout the session

---

### TC-320: Attempt to start video call with offline user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify system handles offline callee by showing a clear message and not building a call

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Callee is offline
- Caller has stable internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Caller selects an offline callee from contact list | Callee appears offline status |
| 2 | 2. Caller clicks the 'Start Video Call' button | System prevents call setup and shows a clear 'User not reachable' message |

**Final Expected Result:** No call is established and a clear offline message is displayed

---

### TC-321: Network degradation triggers adaptive video quality while keeping encryption

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify video quality is reduced when bandwidth drops below minimum while encryption remains active

**Preconditions:**
- Caller and callee are logged in and in an active encrypted video call
- Network shaping tool available to simulate bandwidth drop

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start an encrypted video call between two online users | Encrypted video call is established successfully |
| 2 | 2. Reduce bandwidth below the minimum threshold during the call | System detects degraded network quality |
| 3 | 3. Observe video quality settings/indicators | Video quality is automatically reduced to maintain call stability |
| 4 | 4. Verify encryption indicator after the reduction | Encryption remains active throughout the quality adaptation |

**Final Expected Result:** Video quality adapts to low bandwidth and encryption stays enabled

---

### TC-322: Start video call without camera permission

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify system behavior when camera permission is denied

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Callee is online
- Camera permission is denied for the app

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Caller attempts to start a video call | System prompts for camera permission or shows permission error |
| 2 | 2. Caller keeps permission denied | Call does not start or starts with video disabled and a clear warning |

**Final Expected Result:** User is informed about missing camera permission and call behavior is handled gracefully

---

### TC-323: Start video call without microphone permission

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify system behavior when microphone permission is denied

**Preconditions:**
- Caller is logged in
- Callee is online
- Microphone permission is denied for the app

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Caller attempts to start a video call | System prompts for microphone permission or shows permission error |
| 2 | 2. Caller keeps permission denied | Call does not start or starts with audio disabled and a clear warning |

**Final Expected Result:** User is informed about missing microphone permission and call behavior is handled gracefully

---

### TC-324: Video call cannot be started when caller is offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify system prevents call setup when caller has no internet connection

- Caller is logged in
- Caller has no internet connection
- Callee is online

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Caller attempts to start a video call | System detects no network and blocks call attempt |
| 2 | 2. Observe error messaging | Clear message indicates no internet connection |

### TC-325: Encryption indicator visible throughout the call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify encryption status indicator remains visible and active during the call

**Preconditions:**
- Caller and callee are in an active video call
- Call is established successfully

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Verify encryption indicator at call start | Indicator shows encryption enabled |
| 2 | 2. Keep the call running for several minutes | Indicator remains visible with encryption enabled |
| 3 | 3. End the call | Call ends successfully with no encryption errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Encryption indicator is consistently active during the call

### TC-326: Boundary: Bandwidth just above minimum threshold

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify no unnecessary quality degradation occurs when bandwidth is slightly above the minimum

**Preconditions:**
- Caller and callee are in an active encrypted video call
- Network shaping tool available to set bandwidth just above threshold

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start an encrypted video call | Call is established and encryption is active |
| 2 | 2. Set bandwidth to just above minimum threshold | System maintains current video quality |
| 3 | 3. Monitor video quality indicators | No automatic downscaling occurs |

**Final Expected Result:** Video quality remains stable without unnecessary degradation

---

### TC-327: Boundary: Bandwidth exactly at minimum threshold

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify system behavior at the minimum bandwidth threshold

**Preconditions:**
- Caller and callee are in an active encrypted video call
- Network shaping tool available to set bandwidth to minimum threshold

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start an encrypted video call | Call is established and encryption is active |
| 2 | 2. Set bandwidth to the exact minimum threshold | System either maintains or slightly adjusts video quality as per design |
| 3 | 3. Verify encryption status | Encryption remains active |

**Final Expected Result:** Call remains stable at minimum bandwidth with encryption preserved

### TC-328: Negative: Attempt to start video call while not logged in

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify user must be authenticated to start a video call

### TC-329: Performance: Call setup time under normal conditions

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-036
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-002

**Description:** Verify call establishment time meets performance expectations under stable network

- Caller and callee are logged in and online
- Stable internet connection

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start a video call from caller to callee | Call setup begins immediately |
| 2 | 2. Measure time from call start to call established | Call is established within the defined acceptable time threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** Call setup time is within acceptable performance limits

### TC-330: Create group voice call with multiple participants

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can initiate a group voice call with at least two contacts and invited participants can join

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least two contacts available
- Network connectivity is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contacts list | Contacts list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select two or more contacts | Selected contacts are highlighted/added to the invite list |
| 3 | 3. Choose 'Start Group Voice Call' | Call initiation screen is displayed with selected participants |
| 4 | 4. Confirm to start the call | Group voice call is created and invitations are sent |
| 5 | 5. Have an invited participant accept the call | Participant joins the ongoing group call successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Group voice call is created and multiple participants can join successfully

---

### TC-331: Create group video call with multiple participants

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can initiate a group video call with multiple contacts and invited participants can join

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least two contacts available
- Camera and microphone permissions are granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contacts list | Contacts list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select two or more contacts | Selected contacts are highlighted/added to the invite list |
| 3 | 3. Choose 'Start Group Video Call' | Video call initiation screen is displayed with selected participants |
| 4 | 4. Confirm to start the call | Group video call is created and invitations are sent |
| 5 | 5. Have an invited participant accept the call | Participant joins the ongoing group video call with video/audio enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Group video call is created and multiple participants can join successfully

---

### TC-332: Invite additional participant during ongoing group call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a new participant can be invited during an active call and can join

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An ongoing group call exists with at least two participants
- User has additional contacts available to invite

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the in-call participant panel | Participant panel is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select 'Add participant' | Contact selection UI is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Choose an additional contact and confirm invite | Invitation is sent to the selected contact |
| 4 | 4. Have the invited contact accept the invitation | New participant joins the ongoing call |

**Final Expected Result:** The additional participant receives the invitation and joins the ongoing call

---

### TC-333: Error when starting group call with no participants selected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify system blocks group call creation when no participants are selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on group call initiation screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure no contacts are selected | Invite list remains empty |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Start Group Call' | System displays an error message indicating at least one participant must be selected |

**Final Expected Result:** Group call is not created and a validation error is shown

---

### TC-334: Boundary: start group call with exactly one participant

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify a group call can start with exactly one participant selected (minimum allowed)

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least one contact available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select exactly one contact | One contact is added to the invite list |
| 2 | 2. Choose 'Start Group Call' | Call initiation screen is displayed with one participant |
| 3 | 3. Confirm to start the call | Invitation is sent and call is created |
| 4 | 4. Have the invited contact accept the call | Participant joins successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Group call is created with the single selected participant

---

### TC-335: Invite non-available contact during call

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify proper handling when inviting a contact who is unavailable/offline

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An ongoing group call exists
- At least one contact is offline or unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the in-call participant panel | Participant panel is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an offline/unavailable contact and send invite | System sends invitation and indicates pending/unavailable status |
| 3 | 3. Wait for the invitation timeout period | System shows that the invite was not accepted or expired |

**Final Expected Result:** System handles unavailable participant gracefully and does not disrupt the call

---

### TC-336: Prevent duplicate invitations to the same participant

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify system prevents sending multiple simultaneous invites to the same contact

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- An ongoing group call exists
- A contact has already been invited but not yet joined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to invite the same contact again | System blocks duplicate invite or shows a message indicating an invite is already pending |

**Final Expected Result:** Duplicate invitations are prevented or clearly indicated

---

### TC-337: UI/UX validation of error message for empty selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify the error message content and visibility when no participants are selected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on group call initiation screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click 'Start Group Call' without selecting any participant | Error message is displayed near the selection area |
| 2 | 2. Read the error message | Message explicitly states at least one participant must be selected |

**Final Expected Result:** Clear, accurate error message is displayed for empty participant selection

---

### TC-338: Performance: group call creation time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-037
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-003

**Description:** Verify group call creation and invitation dispatch occur within acceptable time

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least two contacts available
- Network connectivity is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select two or more contacts | Selected contacts are added to the invite list |
| 2 | 2. Start the group call and measure time to call creation | Call is created and invitations are sent within the defined SLA |

**Final Expected Result:** Group call creation and invitation dispatch meet performance SLA

---

### TC-339: Start screen sharing with permission during active call

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that a participant can start sharing a selected screen/window when permission is granted.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- User has screen sharing permission in the app
- OS-level screen capture permission is granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Share Screen' button in the call controls | Screen share selection dialog appears |
| 2 | 2. Select a specific screen or window and confirm | Screen sharing starts and the shared content is shown in the local preview |
| 3 | 3. Ask a remote participant to confirm visibility | Remote participant sees the selected screen/window in real time |

**Final Expected Result:** The selected screen/window is shared with all participants and remains active.

---

### TC-340: Stop screen sharing returns participants to standard view

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that stopping screen sharing ends sharing and returns participants to normal call view.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- User is currently sharing a screen/window

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Stop Sharing' button | Screen sharing stops for the local user |
| 2 | 2. Observe remote participant's call view | Remote participant view returns to the standard call layout |

**Final Expected Result:** Screen sharing ends and all participants return to standard call view.

---

### TC-341: Attempt to start screen sharing without OS permission

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify error handling when OS-level screen capture permission is denied.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- OS-level screen capture permission is denied

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Share Screen' button in the call controls | System prompts for OS permission or indicates permission is missing |
| 2 | 2. Deny the OS permission request (if prompted) | An error message is displayed indicating screen sharing cannot start |

**Final Expected Result:** Screen sharing does not start and a clear error is shown.

---

### TC-342: Cancel screen share selection dialog

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that canceling the selection dialog does not start sharing.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- OS-level screen capture permission is granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Share Screen' button | Screen share selection dialog appears |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Cancel' or close the dialog | Dialog closes and sharing does not start |

**Final Expected Result:** No screen sharing is started and the call view remains unchanged.

---

### TC-343: Switch between window and full screen sharing

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that selecting different share sources (window vs screen) shares the chosen source.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- OS-level screen capture permission is granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Share Screen' button | Screen share selection dialog appears |
| 2 | 2. Select a specific application window and confirm | Selected window is shared |
| 3 | 3. Stop sharing | Sharing stops and call view returns to standard |
| 4 | 4. Click 'Share Screen' again and select an entire screen | Selected screen is shared |

**Final Expected Result:** The system shares the selected source each time without errors.

---

### TC-344: Attempt to start sharing when no call is active

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that screen sharing cannot start outside an active call.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- No active call is ongoing

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the call UI and attempt to click 'Share Screen' | The option is disabled or an informative message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Screen sharing does not start when no call is active.

---

### TC-345: Multiple participants receive shared screen

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that screen sharing is visible to all participants in a multi-party call.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least two remote participants
- OS-level screen capture permission is granted

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start screen sharing by selecting a screen/window | Sharing starts and local preview appears |
| 2 | 2. Verify with each remote participant that the shared content is visible | All participants see the shared content in real time |

**Final Expected Result:** The shared screen is visible to all participants without discrepancy.

---

### TC-346: Performance: screen sharing initiation time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify that screen sharing starts within an acceptable time threshold.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- OS-level screen capture permission is granted
- Network conditions are stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click 'Share Screen' and select a screen/window | Sharing initiation begins immediately |
| 2 | 2. Measure time from confirmation to remote participant seeing the share | Shared content is visible within the defined SLA (e.g., ≤ 3 seconds) |

**Final Expected Result:** Screen sharing starts within the acceptable performance threshold.

---

### TC-347: Revoke OS permission during active sharing

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-038
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-004

**Description:** Verify system behavior if OS permission is revoked while sharing is active.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A call is active with at least one other participant
- User is currently sharing a screen/window

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Revoke OS-level screen capture permission during sharing | System detects permission change or share stream interruption |
| 2 | 2. Observe the call UI and remote participant view | Sharing stops and an error or notification is shown to the sharer |

**Final Expected Result:** Screen sharing ends and the user is informed of the permission issue.

---

### TC-348: Generate call link for scheduled call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify organizer can generate a unique call link for a scheduled call

**Preconditions:**
- Organizer is logged in
- A call is scheduled in the system with status 'Scheduled'

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to the scheduled call details page | Call details are displayed with an option to request a call link |
| 2 | Click on 'Generate Call Link' | System generates a link and displays it to the organizer |
| 3 | Copy the displayed call link | Link is copied successfully and remains visible for the organizer |

**Final Expected Result:** A unique call link is generated and displayed for the scheduled call

---

### TC-349: Verify call link uniqueness across different calls

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure each scheduled call has a unique call link

**Preconditions:**
- Organizer is logged in
- Two different calls are scheduled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Generate a call link for the first scheduled call | A call link is generated and displayed |
| 2 | Generate a call link for the second scheduled call | A call link is generated and displayed |
| 3 | Compare the two generated links | The links are different |

**Final Expected Result:** Each scheduled call has a unique call link

---

### TC-350: Participant joins correct call using link at scheduled time

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify that participants are directed to the correct call when using the link at scheduled time

**Preconditions:**
- A call is scheduled and a link has been generated
- Current time is within the scheduled call time window

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open the call link as a participant | System validates the link and time |
| 2 | Proceed to join the call | Participant is taken to the correct call lobby or meeting room |

**Final Expected Result:** Participant joins the correct scheduled call using the link

---

### TC-351: Prevent link generation for canceled call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure the system blocks link generation for canceled calls

**Preconditions:**
- Organizer is logged in
- A call exists with status 'Canceled'

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to the canceled call details page | Call details are displayed |
| 2 | Attempt to generate a call link | System blocks the action |

**Final Expected Result:** Link generation is prevented and the organizer is informed that the call is canceled

---

### TC-352: Link generation request for already generated link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify the system behavior when organizer requests a link that already exists

**Preconditions:**
- Organizer is logged in
- A call is scheduled and a link has already been generated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to the scheduled call details page | Call details are displayed with the existing link |
| 2 | Click on 'Generate Call Link' again | System shows the existing link or confirms it is already generated |

**Final Expected Result:** System does not create a duplicate link and displays the existing link

---

### TC-353: Participant uses link before scheduled time (boundary condition)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify system behavior when participant uses the link too early

**Preconditions:**
- A call is scheduled and a link has been generated
- Current time is before the scheduled start time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open the call link as a participant | System validates the link and scheduled time |

**Final Expected Result:** Participant is informed that the call has not started yet or is placed in a waiting state

---

### TC-354: Participant uses link after scheduled time window ends (boundary condition)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify system behavior when participant uses the link after the call is over

**Preconditions:**
- A call is scheduled and a link has been generated
- Current time is after the scheduled end time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open the call link as a participant | System validates the link and scheduled time |

**Final Expected Result:** Participant is informed that the call has ended and cannot join

---

### TC-355: Unauthorized user attempts to generate call link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure only organizers can generate call links

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as a participant
- A call is scheduled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to the scheduled call details page | Call details are displayed |
| 2 | Attempt to generate a call link | System denies access or hides the option |

**Final Expected Result:** Non-organizer users cannot generate call links

---

### TC-356: Invalid or tampered call link

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Verify system handling of invalid or altered call link

**Preconditions:**
- A call is scheduled and a link has been generated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Modify the link URL parameters or identifier | Modified link is prepared |
| 2 | Open the modified link as a participant | System validates the link and detects invalidity |

**Final Expected Result:** System shows an error indicating the link is invalid or expired

---

### TC-357: Performance of call link generation

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-039
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-005

**Description:** Ensure call link generation responds within acceptable time

**Preconditions:**
- Organizer is logged in
- A call is scheduled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Click on 'Generate Call Link' and measure response time | Link is generated within the defined SLA (e.g., <= 2 seconds) |

**Final Expected Result:** Call link generation completes within acceptable performance limits

---

### TC-358: Reject incoming call with selected quick response (happy path)

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that selecting a configured quick response and rejecting the call sends the message to the caller.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- Quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI with quick response options is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Select a quick response message. | Selected quick response is highlighted/selected. |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected and the selected message is sent to the caller. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined and the selected quick response is delivered to the caller.

---

### TC-359: Reject incoming call without selecting quick response

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that rejecting a call without selecting a quick response sends no message.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- Quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Do not select any quick response. | No quick response is selected. |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected and no message is sent. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined and no message is delivered to the caller.

---

### TC-360: Reject call with quick response when messaging service is unavailable

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that the call is rejected and user is informed when message delivery fails.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- Quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI with quick response options is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Select a quick response message. | Selected quick response is highlighted/selected. |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected and a notification informs the user that the message could not be sent. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined and the user is informed that the quick response message failed to send.

---

### TC-361: Reject call with empty quick response list

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify behavior when no quick responses are configured.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- No quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI shows no quick response options. |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected and no message is sent. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined without sending any message.

---

### TC-362: Change selected quick response before rejecting call

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify that the last selected quick response is the one sent.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- Quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI with quick response options is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Select quick response A. | Quick response A is highlighted/selected. |
| 3 | 3. Select quick response B. | Quick response B is highlighted/selected and A is deselected. |
| 4 | 4. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected and quick response B is sent to the caller. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is declined and the last selected quick response is delivered.

---

### TC-363: Reject call while message send is delayed

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-040
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-006

**Description:** Verify call rejection is immediate even if message delivery is slow.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Incoming call is displayed
- Quick responses are configured
- Messaging service is available but slow (simulated latency)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the incoming call screen. | Incoming call UI with quick response options is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Select a quick response message. | Selected quick response is highlighted/selected. |
| 3 | 3. Tap 'Reject' to decline the call. | Call is rejected immediately; message sending proceeds asynchronously. |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is rejected without delay and message delivery completes when service responds.

---

### TC-364: Display call history with chronological order and direction

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that call history shows a chronological list with date/time and call direction for a customer with calls

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Customer has at least 3 call records with varying dates/times and directions (inbound/outbound)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | Call History view loads without errors |
| 3 | 3. Observe the list of calls | Calls are displayed in chronological order with visible date/time and call direction for each entry |

**Final Expected Result:** A complete chronological list of calls with date/time and direction is displayed

---

### TC-365: Empty state when no call history exists

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that the system shows an empty state for a customer with no call history

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Customer has zero call records

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | Call History view loads without errors |
| 3 | 3. Observe the call history content | Empty state is displayed indicating no call history is available |

**Final Expected Result:** An empty state message is shown and no call records are listed

---

### TC-366: Error handling when call history retrieval fails

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that an error message is shown and no incomplete data is displayed when retrieval fails

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Call history service is configured to return an error for the customer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | System attempts to load call history |
| 3 | 3. Observe the call history area | Error message is displayed and no call records are shown |

**Final Expected Result:** An error message is displayed and no incomplete or incorrect call data is visible

---

### TC-367: Chronological order boundary with same timestamp

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify ordering behavior when multiple calls have identical timestamps

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Customer has at least 2 call records with the same date/time but different directions

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | Call History view loads without errors |
| 3 | 3. Inspect the ordering of calls with identical timestamps | Calls with identical timestamps are displayed consistently based on defined secondary sorting (e.g., creation order or ID) |

**Final Expected Result:** Call entries with identical timestamps are displayed in a deterministic, consistent order with correct date/time and direction

---

### TC-368: Call history shows correct direction labels

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that each call entry displays the correct direction (inbound/outbound)

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Customer has at least one inbound and one outbound call record

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | Call History view loads without errors |
| 3 | 3. Compare displayed directions with known call records | Each call entry shows the correct inbound/outbound direction |

**Final Expected Result:** All call entries display accurate direction labels

---

### TC-369: Integration: Call history retrieval from backend

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that the UI correctly consumes and renders the call history API response

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Backend API returns a valid list of call records for the customer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | UI sends request to call history API |
| 3 | 3. Validate UI list against API response fields | UI displays each call's date/time and direction exactly as returned by the API |

**Final Expected Result:** UI accurately renders call history data retrieved from the backend

---

### TC-370: Performance: Call history loads within acceptable time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Verify that call history loads promptly for a customer with many calls

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Customer has 500+ call records

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section and start timing | System begins loading call history |
| 3 | 3. Stop timing when the call list renders | Call history renders within the defined performance threshold (e.g., <= 2 seconds) |

**Final Expected Result:** Call history loads within the acceptable performance threshold without errors

---

### TC-371: Negative: Prevent display of partial data when retrieval fails mid-load

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-041
**Requirement:** WA-CALL-007

**Description:** Ensure no partial call entries are shown if the call history request fails during loading

**Preconditions:**
- Agent is logged in
- Network is configured to fail mid-response for call history API

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the customer's profile | Customer profile loads successfully |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to the Call History section | System attempts to load call history |
| 3 | 3. Simulate mid-load failure | Error message is displayed and any partially loaded call entries are cleared or not shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Error message is shown and no partial or incorrect data is displayed

---

### TC-372: Create status with valid content

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a logged-in user can create a status with valid content and it becomes visible for 24 hours

**Preconditions:**
- User is authenticated
- User is on the status creation interface

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter valid status content within allowed limits | Content is accepted by the input field |
| 2 | 2. Submit the status | Submission is processed without errors |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to the status feed or profile status area | Newly created status is visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved and visible for the next 24 hours

---

### TC-373: Reject status creation without required content

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify the system rejects submission when required content is missing

**Preconditions:**
- User is authenticated
- User is on the status creation interface

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave the status content empty | Input field remains empty |
| 2 | 2. Submit the status | Submission is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Validation message is displayed and status is not created

---

### TC-374: Status expires after 24 hours

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a status created more than 24 hours ago is no longer displayed

**Preconditions:**
- A status exists that was created more than 24 hours ago
- Any user is viewing statuses

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the status feed or profile status area | Statuses load |
| 2 | 2. Search for the status created over 24 hours ago | The expired status is not displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Statuses older than 24 hours are not visible

---

### TC-375: Boundary: status visible just before 24-hour expiry

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a status is still visible shortly before 24 hours have elapsed

**Preconditions:**
- A status exists created 23 hours and 59 minutes ago
- Any user is viewing statuses

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the status feed or profile status area | Statuses load |
| 2 | 2. Locate the status created 23 hours and 59 minutes ago | The status is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is visible until the 24-hour expiry threshold

---

### TC-376: Boundary: status not visible at 24-hour mark

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a status becomes hidden immediately after the 24-hour threshold

**Preconditions:**
- A status exists created exactly 24 hours ago
- Any user is viewing statuses

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the status feed or profile status area | Statuses load |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to locate the status created exactly 24 hours ago | The status is not displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is not visible at or beyond 24 hours

---

### TC-377: Reject status with content exceeding maximum length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify system validation when status content exceeds allowed length

**Preconditions:**
- User is authenticated
- User is on the status creation interface
- Maximum content length is defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter content exceeding the maximum length | Input is accepted but marked as invalid or limited |
| 2 | 2. Submit the status | Submission is rejected |

**Final Expected Result:** Validation message is displayed and status is not created

---

### TC-378: Create status with minimum valid content

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify a status can be created with the minimum required content length

**Preconditions:**
- User is authenticated
- User is on the status creation interface
- Minimum content length is defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter content exactly at the minimum allowed length | Content is accepted by the input field |
| 2 | 2. Submit the status | Submission is processed without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is saved and visible for 24 hours

---

### TC-379: Unauthorized user cannot access status creation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify unauthenticated users cannot create a status

**Preconditions:**
- User is not authenticated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the status creation interface URL | User is redirected to login or shown access denied |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to submit a status | Submission is blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Unauthenticated users cannot create a status

---

### TC-380: Status persistence and visibility across sessions

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify status remains visible to other users within 24 hours across sessions

**Preconditions:**
- User A is authenticated and creates a status with valid content
- User B is authenticated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A submits a valid status | Status is saved |
| 2 | 2. User B opens the status feed | User A's status is visible within 24 hours |

**Final Expected Result:** Status is visible to other users for 24 hours

---

### TC-381: System removes expired status from feed

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-042
**Requirement:** WA-STS-001

**Description:** Verify expired status is removed from feed after 24 hours without manual refresh logic issues

**Preconditions:**
- A status exists created 23 hours and 50 minutes ago
- Any user is viewing the status feed

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Confirm the status is currently visible | Status is displayed in the feed |
| 2 | 2. Wait until the status age exceeds 24 hours and refresh the feed | Expired status is no longer displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Expired status is removed from the feed after 24 hours

---

### TC-382: Display defined contact status in details view

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

- A contact exists with status set to 'Available'

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contacts list | Contacts list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the contact with status 'Available' | Contact details view opens |

**Final Expected Result:** Contact status field displays 'Available'

---

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

- User is registered and logged in
- A contact exists without a defined status

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contacts list | Contacts list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the contact without a status | Contact details view opens |

**Final Expected Result:** Contact status field displays default label 'Unknown'

### TC-384: Show error when status retrieval fails

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-043
**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

|---|--------|------------------|

**Final Expected Result:** An error message indicates the status is unavailable

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

**Status:** manual

**Requirement:** WA-STS-002

|---|--------|------------------|
