### TC-492: Send HD-capable media in HD quality successfully

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify HD media is delivered in HD quality with stable network

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an HD-capable media file
- Recipient exists and can receive media
- Network bandwidth is stable and sufficient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an HD-capable media file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Choose quality option as HD | HD option is selected and enabled |
| 4 | 4. Send the media to the recipient | Send action is initiated without errors |
| 5 | 5. Confirm delivery on recipient side | Recipient receives media marked and rendered as HD |

**Final Expected Result:** Media is delivered in HD quality to the recipient

---

### TC-493: Prevent HD selection for non-HD media

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify system blocks HD selection for non-HD files and informs user

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has a non-HD media file
- Network bandwidth is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a non-HD media file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to select HD quality | HD option is disabled or blocked |
| 4 | 4. Observe system message | User is informed that the file does not support HD |

**Final Expected Result:** HD selection is prevented and user is informed about unsupported HD

---

### TC-494: Insufficient network bandwidth triggers queue option

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify user is notified and can queue the send when bandwidth is insufficient

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an HD-capable media file
- Network bandwidth is insufficient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an HD-capable media file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Select HD quality and send | System detects low bandwidth |
| 4 | 4. Choose the queue option when prompted | Media send is queued |

**Final Expected Result:** User is notified of insufficient bandwidth and media is queued for sending

---

### TC-495: Insufficient network bandwidth offers downgrade option

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify user can downgrade quality when bandwidth is insufficient

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an HD-capable media file
- Network bandwidth is insufficient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an HD-capable media file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Select HD quality and send | System detects low bandwidth and prompts options |
| 4 | 4. Select the downgrade option | Quality changes to a lower level (e.g., SD) |
| 5 | 5. Confirm send | Media is sent at downgraded quality |

**Final Expected Result:** User is notified and media is sent at downgraded quality when bandwidth is insufficient

---

### TC-496: Boundary: HD file at minimum HD resolution is accepted

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify media at minimum HD threshold can be sent in HD

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has a media file at minimum HD resolution threshold
- Network bandwidth is stable and sufficient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the minimum-threshold HD file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Select HD quality | HD option is enabled and selected |
| 4 | 4. Send the media | Send action is initiated without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Media is sent in HD quality for minimum-threshold HD files

---

### TC-497: Boundary: file just below HD threshold is blocked

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify media just below HD resolution is not allowed to send in HD

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has a media file just below HD resolution threshold
- Network bandwidth is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the sub-threshold media file | Selected media appears in the preview |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to select HD quality | HD option is disabled or blocked |
| 4 | 4. Observe system message | User is informed that the file does not support HD |

**Final Expected Result:** HD selection is blocked for media below HD threshold

---

### TC-498: HD selection persists across media change

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify HD selection state updates correctly when switching between HD and non-HD files

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has one HD-capable file and one non-HD file
- Network bandwidth is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the HD-capable file and choose HD quality | HD option is selected and enabled |
| 3 | 3. Switch to the non-HD file | HD option becomes disabled or unselected |
| 4 | 4. Switch back to the HD-capable file | HD option becomes enabled again |

**Final Expected Result:** HD selection state correctly reflects the currently selected file capability

---

### TC-499: User notified when HD delivery fails mid-send due to bandwidth drop

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-056
**Requirement:** WA-MED-010

**Description:** Verify user is notified and offered options if bandwidth drops during send

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an HD-capable media file
- Network bandwidth starts stable and then drops below threshold during send

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the media sharing screen | Media sharing options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an HD-capable media file and choose HD quality | HD option is selected and enabled |
| 3 | 3. Start sending the media | Send begins successfully |
| 4 | 4. Simulate bandwidth drop during send | System detects insufficient bandwidth |
| 5 | 5. Select queue or downgrade when prompted | System applies the chosen option and informs the user |

**Final Expected Result:** User is notified of bandwidth drop and can queue or downgrade the send

---

### TC-500: E2E encryption with valid keys (sender -> recipient)

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify message is encrypted on sender device and only recipient can decrypt

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient accounts exist and are logged in on separate devices
- Sender and recipient have valid, exchanged encryption keys
- Network connectivity is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On sender device, compose a new message to the recipient with known plaintext | Message composer accepts input |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is queued/sent successfully |
| 3 | 3. Inspect outgoing payload on sender device before transmission | Payload is encrypted and does not contain plaintext |
| 4 | 4. On recipient device, open the conversation | Recipient receives the message |
| 5 | 5. Attempt to read the message content | Message is decrypted and readable on recipient device |

**Final Expected Result:** Message is encrypted on sender and only decrypted on recipient device

---

### TC-501: Server stores/transmits only encrypted payload

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify server cannot decrypt and stores only encrypted message content

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid encryption keys
- Server logging/monitoring access is available
- A message has been sent from sender to recipient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a message from sender to recipient | Message is accepted by the system |
| 2 | 2. Inspect server-side stored message record for payload | Stored payload is encrypted and does not reveal plaintext |
| 3 | 3. Inspect server logs/processing pipeline for message content | No plaintext appears; only encrypted payloads are processed |

**Final Expected Result:** Server stores/transmits encrypted payloads only and cannot decrypt content

---

### TC-502: Recipient without valid decryption key cannot read message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify decryption fails and proper error is shown when recipient lacks valid key

**Preconditions:**
- Sender has valid encryption key
- Recipient is missing or has invalid decryption key for the message
- A message has been sent to recipient

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On recipient device, open the conversation containing the message | Message appears as encrypted or unreadable |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to decrypt/read the message | Decryption attempt fails |
| 3 | 3. Observe UI feedback | Error message indicates decryption is not possible |

**Final Expected Result:** Recipient cannot decrypt the message and receives an appropriate error

---

### TC-503: Message integrity: tampered ciphertext cannot be decrypted

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify that altered encrypted payload cannot be decrypted by recipient

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- Ability to simulate tampering of ciphertext in transit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a message from sender to recipient | Message is sent successfully |
| 2 | 2. Tamper with the encrypted payload before it reaches recipient | Tampered payload is delivered |
| 3 | 3. On recipient device, attempt to read the message | Decryption fails due to integrity check |
| 4 | 4. Observe UI feedback | Error indicates message is corrupted or cannot be decrypted |

**Final Expected Result:** Tampered ciphertext cannot be decrypted and appropriate error is shown

---

### TC-504: Key rotation: new keys used for new messages

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify messages sent after key rotation are encrypted with new keys and decryptable by recipient

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- Key rotation capability is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Rotate sender and recipient keys | New keys are generated and exchanged |
| 2 | 2. Send a new message after rotation | Message is sent successfully |
| 3 | 3. On recipient device, read the new message | Message is decrypted successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Messages after key rotation are encrypted with new keys and decrypt correctly

---

### TC-505: Backward compatibility: old messages remain decryptable after key rotation

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify messages encrypted before key rotation can still be decrypted

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- At least one message exists prior to key rotation

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Rotate keys for sender and recipient | Key rotation completes successfully |
| 2 | 2. On recipient device, open conversation with pre-rotation message | Pre-rotation message is visible |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to read the pre-rotation message | Message decrypts successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Messages sent before rotation remain decryptable

---

### TC-506: Boundary: Empty message encryption

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify encryption/decryption behavior for an empty message

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- System allows sending empty messages or handles validation

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. On sender device, attempt to send an empty message | System either prevents sending or sends an encrypted empty payload |
| 2 | 2. If sent, on recipient device open the message | Message decrypts to empty content or appropriate validation message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Empty message is handled securely without exposing plaintext

---

### TC-507: Boundary: Maximum message size encryption

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify encryption and decryption at maximum allowed message length

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- Maximum message size is defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Compose a message at the maximum allowed size | Composer accepts the maximum size message |
| 2 | 2. Send the message | Message is sent successfully without errors |
| 3 | 3. On recipient device, read the message | Message decrypts successfully within acceptable time |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum size message is encrypted/decrypted successfully and within performance limits

---

### TC-508: Multiple recipients: only intended recipients can decrypt

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify that only recipients included in the message can decrypt it

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and two recipients have valid keys
- A third user exists without being included in recipients

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a message to Recipient A and Recipient B | Message is sent successfully |
| 2 | 2. Recipient A opens and reads the message | Message decrypts successfully |
| 3 | 3. Recipient B opens and reads the message | Message decrypts successfully |
| 4 | 4. Non-recipient user attempts to access the message | Message cannot be decrypted or accessed |

**Final Expected Result:** Only intended recipients can decrypt the message

---

### TC-509: Network retry: ciphertext remains encrypted during resend

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-057
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-001

**Description:** Verify encrypted payload persists across retries and is not re-sent as plaintext

**Preconditions:**
- Sender and recipient have valid keys
- Network instability can be simulated

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send a message while simulating intermittent network failures | Message retry mechanism is triggered |
| 2 | 2. Inspect outbound payload during retries | Payload remains encrypted and does not contain plaintext |
| 3 | 3. Once delivered, recipient reads the message | Message decrypts successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Retries do not expose plaintext; encryption remains intact

---

### TC-510: Successful manual verification of valid encryption security code

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify that a valid code can be manually verified and recorded with timestamp and verifier

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with a valid encryption security code
- Admin has permission to verify encryption security codes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Asset details page loads with security code section visible |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Manual Verify' action | Verification prompt or workflow is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm verification action | System processes verification and shows success confirmation |
| 4 | 4. Open verification history or audit log | A success entry is recorded with current timestamp and verifier identity |

**Final Expected Result:** The system verifies the code successfully and records the verification with timestamp and verifier

---

### TC-511: Manual verification rejects invalid/mismatched code

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify that invalid or mismatched code results in rejection, error message, and failure record

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with an invalid or mismatched encryption security code
- Admin has permission to verify encryption security codes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Asset details page loads with security code section visible |
| 2 | 2. Click on 'Manual Verify' action | Verification prompt or workflow is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm verification action | System rejects verification and displays a clear error message |
| 4 | 4. Open verification history or audit log | A failure entry is recorded with timestamp and verifier identity |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification is rejected with an error message and failure is recorded

---

### TC-512: Manual verification blocked when no security code exists

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure verification is prevented if no encryption security code is available

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists without an encryption security code
- Admin has permission to verify encryption security codes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Asset details page loads and indicates no security code is available |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to initiate 'Manual Verify' | System prevents verification and displays message indicating no code is available |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification is blocked and user is informed that no code is available

---

### TC-513: Audit log captures verifier identity accurately

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify the recorded verifier matches the logged-in administrator performing verification

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator A is logged in
- Stored asset exists with a valid encryption security code
- Admin A has permission to verify encryption security codes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Asset details page loads with security code section visible |
| 2 | 2. Initiate and confirm manual verification | System shows successful verification |
| 3 | 3. Open verification history or audit log | Verifier identity matches administrator A |

**Final Expected Result:** Audit log correctly records the verifier identity

---

### TC-514: Timestamp recorded at verification time

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure the verification timestamp is within acceptable time window of action

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with a valid encryption security code
- Admin has permission to verify encryption security codes

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Capture current system time | Current time is noted for comparison |
| 2 | 2. Initiate and confirm manual verification | System shows successful verification |
| 3 | 3. Open verification history or audit log and view timestamp | Timestamp is within an acceptable window (e.g., ±1 minute) of noted time |

**Final Expected Result:** Verification timestamp is accurate and within acceptable time window

---

### TC-515: Verification action requires proper permissions

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Validate that a non-authorized user cannot perform manual verification

**Preconditions:**
- Non-admin user is logged in
- Stored asset exists with a valid encryption security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Asset details page loads |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to initiate 'Manual Verify' | System denies access and displays permission error |

**Final Expected Result:** Manual verification is blocked for users without permission

---

### TC-516: UI prevents verification when no code is available (boundary condition)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure the 'Manual Verify' action is disabled or hidden when no code exists

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists without an encryption security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the stored asset details page | Security code section indicates no code |
| 2 | 2. Observe the 'Manual Verify' control state | Control is disabled or hidden with explanatory text |

**Final Expected Result:** UI state prevents initiating verification when no code is present

---

### TC-517: Failure record created for invalid code verification attempt

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Confirm failure is persisted for auditing when verification fails

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with an invalid or mismatched encryption security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate manual verification on the asset | System displays verification prompt |
| 2 | 2. Confirm verification action | System rejects verification and shows error |
| 3 | 3. Query verification history via UI or API | Failure record exists with reason, timestamp, and verifier |

**Final Expected Result:** Failure is recorded and retrievable from verification history

---

### TC-518: System handles rapid consecutive verification attempts

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Verify system behavior when multiple verification attempts occur in quick succession

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with a valid encryption security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate manual verification and confirm | System responds with success |
| 2 | 2. Immediately initiate verification again within 5 seconds | System handles request without error; response is consistent (success or idempotent handling) |
| 3 | 3. Check verification history | Entries are recorded according to system rules (single or multiple) without corruption |

**Final Expected Result:** System remains stable and logs verification attempts appropriately

---

### TC-519: Error message clarity for invalid code verification

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-058
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-002

**Description:** Ensure the error message is clear and actionable when verification fails

**Preconditions:**
- Security administrator is logged in
- Stored asset exists with an invalid or mismatched encryption security code

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate manual verification on the asset | Verification prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Confirm verification action | System displays a clear error message indicating invalid/mismatched code |

**Final Expected Result:** Error message is clear, specific, and user-friendly

---

### TC-520: Prompt for authentication when app lock enabled

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that authentication is required before showing messages when app lock is enabled

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- Messages exist in the message list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Close the app completely | App is not running |
| 2 | 2. Open the app | Authentication prompt is displayed before any messages |

**Final Expected Result:** User is prompted for authentication and no messages are visible

---

### TC-521: Unlock app with valid authentication

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that valid authentication unlocks the app and shows the message list

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- App is currently locked
- Messages exist in the message list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter valid authentication credentials (e.g., PIN/biometric) | Authentication is accepted |
| 2 | 2. Submit authentication | App unlocks and navigates to message list |

**Final Expected Result:** Message list is displayed after successful authentication

---

### TC-522: Remain locked with invalid authentication

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that invalid authentication does not unlock the app and messages remain hidden

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- App is currently locked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter invalid authentication credentials | Authentication is rejected |
| 2 | 2. Submit authentication | App remains locked and no messages are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** App stays locked and message list is not visible

---

### TC-523: No authentication prompt when app lock disabled

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that disabling app lock allows direct access to messages

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is disabled
- Messages exist in the message list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Close the app completely | App is not running |
| 2 | 2. Open the app | Message list is displayed without authentication prompt |

**Final Expected Result:** Messages are visible immediately when app lock is disabled

---

### TC-524: Boundary: Empty message list after unlocking

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify behavior when there are no messages after successful authentication

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- App is currently locked
- Message list is empty

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter valid authentication credentials | Authentication is accepted |
| 2 | 2. Submit authentication | App unlocks and navigates to message list |

**Final Expected Result:** Empty message list state is displayed without exposing any content

---

### TC-525: Multiple invalid attempts do not reveal messages

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that repeated invalid authentication attempts never show messages

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- App is currently locked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter invalid authentication credentials (attempt 1) | Authentication is rejected |
| 2 | 2. Enter invalid authentication credentials (attempt 2) | Authentication is rejected |
| 3 | 3. Enter invalid authentication credentials (attempt 3) | App remains locked and no messages are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** App remains locked after multiple invalid attempts and messages are not visible

---

### TC-526: Re-lock after app is backgrounded and reopened

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that app lock is enforced when app is reopened

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- User has previously unlocked the app
- Messages exist in the message list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send the app to background | App is running in background |
| 2 | 2. Reopen the app from background | Authentication prompt is displayed before showing messages |

**Final Expected Result:** App requires authentication again before messages are shown

---

### TC-527: Authentication prompt blocks message preview on launch

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-059
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-003

**Description:** Verify that no message content is visible behind the authentication screen

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- App lock is enabled
- Messages exist in the message list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the app | Authentication prompt appears |
| 2 | 2. Observe the screen behind the authentication prompt | No message content or previews are visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Authentication screen fully obscures message content

---

### TC-528: Block an existing contact successfully

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that a user can block a contact and the contact can no longer send messages or requests.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact list | Contact list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the target contact | Contact details view is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Click the block action | System confirms the block action or performs the block |
| 4 | 4. Attempt to receive a message or request from the blocked contact | No messages or requests from the blocked contact are delivered |

**Final Expected Result:** The contact is marked as blocked and can no longer send messages or requests.

---

### TC-529: Prevent duplicate blocking of an already blocked contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that blocking an already blocked contact is prevented and an informative message is shown.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is already blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact list | Contact list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the already blocked contact | Contact details view is displayed with blocked status |
| 3 | 3. Click the block action again | System prevents the action and displays a message that the contact is already blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Duplicate blocking is prevented and the user is informed the contact is already blocked.

---

### TC-530: Handle system error during block action

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that a system error during blocking shows an error message and does not change block status.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is not currently blocked
- System is configured to simulate a block action failure

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the contact list | Contact list is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select the target contact | Contact details view is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Click the block action while the system error is triggered | System displays an error message |
| 4 | 4. Refresh or re-open the contact details | Contact status remains unblocked |

**Final Expected Result:** An error is shown and the contact remains unblocked.

---

### TC-531: Block action from contact details view

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify block action is available and functional from the contact details view.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the contact details view for the target contact | Contact details view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Verify block action is visible | Block action is visible and enabled |
| 3 | 3. Click the block action | Contact is marked as blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Contact is blocked from the details view.

---

### TC-532: Verify blocked status persists after logout/login

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Ensure blocked status is persisted across sessions.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is already blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out of the application | User is logged out successfully |
| 2 | 2. Log back in | User is logged in successfully |
| 3 | 3. Open the blocked contact details | Contact shows blocked status |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocked status persists across sessions.

---

### TC-533: Blocked contact cannot send new messages

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that a blocked contact cannot send messages to the user.

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User B is a contact of User A
- User B is blocked by User A

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. From User B account, send a message to User A | Message send action is rejected or not delivered |
| 2 | 2. From User A account, check inbox or notifications | No message from User B is received |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocked contact cannot deliver messages.

---

### TC-534: Blocked contact cannot send new requests

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that a blocked contact cannot send new requests to the user.

**Preconditions:**
- User A is logged in
- User B is a contact of User A
- User B is blocked by User A

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. From User B account, send a request to User A (e.g., new contact request or invite) | Request send action is rejected or not delivered |
| 2 | 2. From User A account, check requests or notifications | No request from User B is received |

**Final Expected Result:** Blocked contact cannot deliver requests.

---

### TC-535: Boundary: Block action is disabled when no contact is selected

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify that block action cannot be executed without selecting a contact.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has at least one contact in contact list

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to contact list without selecting any contact | No contact is selected |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to click the block action | Block action is disabled or prompts user to select a contact |

**Final Expected Result:** Block action cannot be executed without selecting a contact.

---

### TC-536: UI indicator updates when contact is blocked

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-060
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-004

**Description:** Verify UI shows blocked status after blocking.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has an existing contact in contact list
- Contact is not currently blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the target contact and click block action | Block action completes successfully |
| 2 | 2. Return to contact list | Contact shows a blocked indicator (icon/label) |

**Final Expected Result:** UI accurately reflects blocked status.

### TC-537: Report message with valid reason

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify a user can report a message with a selected reason and the system confirms and records it.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing an existing message that has not been reported by this user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Report' option on the message | Report dialog opens with a list of reasons |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid reason from the list | Selected reason is highlighted/marked |
| 3 | 3. Click the 'Submit' button | Submission is accepted and a confirmation message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Report is created and recorded for review; confirmation is displayed.

---

### TC-538: Report contact with valid reason

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify a user can report a contact with a selected reason and the system confirms and records it.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing an existing contact that has not been reported by this user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Click the 'Report' option on the contact profile | Report dialog opens with a list of reasons |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid reason from the list | Selected reason is highlighted/marked |
| 3 | 3. Click the 'Submit' button | Submission is accepted and a confirmation message is shown |

**Final Expected Result:** Report is created and recorded for review; confirmation is displayed.

---

### TC-539: Prevent reporting without selecting reason

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify system blocks submission when no reason is selected and prompts user to choose a reason.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing an existing message or contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the report dialog | Report dialog opens with list of reasons and submit button |
| 2 | 2. Do not select any reason and click 'Submit' | Submission is blocked and a prompt appears to select a reason |

**Final Expected Result:** No report is created; user is prompted to select a reason.

---

### TC-540: Prevent duplicate report for same message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify system informs user when reporting the same message again and does not create a duplicate report.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has already reported the same message

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the report dialog for the previously reported message | Report dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select any reason and click 'Submit' | System informs user that the message has already been reported |

**Final Expected Result:** No duplicate report is created; user is notified that it was already reported.

---

### TC-541: Prevent duplicate report for same contact

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify system informs user when reporting the same contact again and does not create a duplicate report.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has already reported the same contact

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the report dialog for the previously reported contact | Report dialog opens |
| 2 | 2. Select any reason and click 'Submit' | System informs user that the contact has already been reported |

**Final Expected Result:** No duplicate report is created; user is notified that it was already reported.

---

### TC-542: Boundary: Minimum reason selection (single option)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify submission succeeds when only a single reason is selected (minimum selection).

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing an existing message or contact that has not been reported by this user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the report dialog | Report dialog opens with list of reasons |
| 2 | 2. Select exactly one reason and click 'Submit' | Report is submitted and confirmation is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Report is created successfully with the selected reason.

---

### TC-543: Integration: Report is stored and retrievable for review

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Verify backend records the report for review after submission.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing an existing message or contact that has not been reported by this user

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Submit a report with a selected reason | Confirmation is displayed to the user |
| 2 | 2. Query the review/report storage or admin review list | The submitted report appears with correct user, target, and reason |

**Final Expected Result:** Report is persisted and available for review.

### TC-544: End-to-end: Report message flow from UI to confirmation

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-061
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-005

**Description:** Validate full UI flow for reporting a message including prompt handling.

- User is viewing an existing message that has not been reported by this user

### TC-545: Enable PIN verification with valid PIN

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that a logged-in user can enable PIN verification with a valid PIN and the setting is saved

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is not enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Account Security settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select 'Enable PIN verification' | PIN setup form is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Enter a valid PIN that meets format requirements | PIN input is accepted |
| 4 | 4. Confirm the PIN and save | System saves the PIN and shows success confirmation |

**Final Expected Result:** Additional PIN verification is enabled and stored for the user

---

### TC-546: Protected action with correct PIN

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that a protected action proceeds when the correct PIN is entered

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is enabled
- User knows the correct PIN
- Protected action is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a protected action (e.g., change email) | PIN verification prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter the correct PIN | PIN is accepted |
| 3 | 3. Confirm the action | Protected action completes successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Protected action is allowed with correct PIN

---

### TC-547: Protected action with incorrect PIN

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that a protected action is blocked when an incorrect PIN is entered

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is enabled
- Protected action is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a protected action | PIN verification prompt is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter an incorrect PIN | System displays an error message indicating incorrect PIN |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to proceed with the action | Action remains blocked |

**Final Expected Result:** Protected action is blocked and an error message is shown

---

### TC-548: Disable PIN verification

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that disabling PIN verification removes the additional PIN requirement

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Account Security settings | Security settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select 'Disable PIN verification' | System confirms the change |
| 3 | 3. Initiate a protected action | Action proceeds without a PIN prompt |

**Final Expected Result:** Additional PIN verification is disabled and no PIN is required for protected actions

---

### TC-549: Enable PIN verification with mismatched confirmation PIN

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that the system rejects PIN setup when confirmation does not match

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is not enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Account Security settings and select 'Enable PIN verification' | PIN setup form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a valid PIN | PIN input is accepted |
| 3 | 3. Enter a different PIN in the confirmation field | System shows a mismatch error |
| 4 | 4. Attempt to save | PIN is not saved and verification remains disabled |

**Final Expected Result:** System prevents enabling PIN verification when confirmation PIN does not match

---

### TC-550: Enable PIN verification with invalid PIN format

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that invalid PIN formats are rejected

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is not enabled
- PIN format requirements are defined (e.g., 4 digits)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Account Security settings and select 'Enable PIN verification' | PIN setup form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter an invalid PIN (e.g., too short or non-numeric) | System displays a validation error |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to save | PIN is not saved and verification remains disabled |

**Final Expected Result:** System rejects invalid PIN formats and does not enable verification

---

### TC-551: Protected action after disabling PIN verification

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that previously enabled PIN does not prompt after disabling

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification was enabled and then disabled
- Protected action is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate a protected action | Action proceeds without a PIN prompt |

**Final Expected Result:** No PIN verification is required after disabling the feature

---

### TC-552: Enable PIN verification persists after logout and login

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that the enabled PIN verification setting persists across sessions

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out of the application | User is logged out |
| 2 | 2. Log in again | User is logged in |
| 3 | 3. Initiate a protected action | PIN verification prompt is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** PIN verification remains enabled after re-login

---

### TC-553: Boundary test: Minimum valid PIN length

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-062
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-006

**Description:** Verify that the minimum allowed PIN length is accepted

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Additional PIN verification is not enabled
- PIN minimum length requirement is defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Account Security settings and select 'Enable PIN verification' | PIN setup form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter a PIN at the minimum allowed length and confirm it | PIN is accepted |
| 3 | 3. Save the PIN | System saves the PIN and enables verification |

**Final Expected Result:** Minimum valid PIN length is accepted and verification is enabled

---

### TC-554: Flag and block content that matches spam rules

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify content matching configured spam rules is flagged and not published

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rules include keyword: 'buy now'
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content containing the phrase 'buy now' | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is flagged as spam and not published

---

### TC-555: Accept and publish content that does not match spam rules

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify clean content is accepted and published when spam detection is enabled

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rules include keyword: 'buy now'
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content that does not include any spam keywords | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is accepted and published normally

---

### TC-556: Route content to manual review when spam service is unavailable

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify system handles spam detection service outage by logging error and routing to manual review

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam detection service is unavailable or returns error
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter any content | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content while the spam detection service is down | System attempts to evaluate content and receives an error |

**Final Expected Result:** Error is logged and content is routed to manual review queue, not published

---

### TC-557: Boundary: Content exactly matches spam rule pattern

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify exact match of spam rule triggers spam flag

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rule pattern is exact match: 'FREE OFFER'
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content exactly 'FREE OFFER' | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is flagged as spam and not published

---

### TC-558: Boundary: Content just below spam rule length threshold

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify content below length threshold is not flagged if no other rules match

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rule length threshold is > 2000 characters
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content of 1999 characters with no spam keywords | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is accepted and published normally

---

### TC-559: Boundary: Content just above spam rule length threshold

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify content above length threshold is flagged if rule is configured for excessive length

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rule length threshold is > 2000 characters
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content of 2001 characters | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is flagged as spam and not published

---

### TC-560: Case-insensitive spam rule matching

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify spam detection matches rules regardless of letter case

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam rule keyword is 'Buy Now' (case-insensitive)
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to content submission form | Content submission form is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Enter content containing 'bUy nOw' | Content is entered in the form field |
| 3 | 3. Submit the content | Submission is processed by spam detection |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is flagged as spam and not published

---

### TC-561: Ensure manual review queue entry includes content metadata

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify that when spam service fails, content is queued with required metadata for review

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam detection service returns error
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Submit content while spam detection service is failing | System fails to evaluate content and routes it to manual review |
| 2 | 2. Open manual review queue | Manual review queue is displayed |
| 3 | 3. Locate the submitted content entry | Entry exists with metadata (user ID, timestamp, content snippet) |

**Final Expected Result:** Content is present in manual review queue with required metadata and not published

---

### TC-562: Logging of spam service errors

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify that spam detection errors are logged when the service is unavailable

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Spam detection service is unavailable
- User is logged in
- Log access is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Submit content while spam detection service is down | System attempts evaluation and fails |
| 2 | 2. Access application logs | Logs are accessible |
| 3 | 3. Search for the spam detection error entry | Error log entry is found with relevant error details |

**Final Expected Result:** Spam detection error is logged and content is routed to manual review

---

### TC-563: Performance: Spam detection evaluation within acceptable time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-063
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-007

**Description:** Verify spam detection evaluation does not exceed response time threshold

**Preconditions:**
- Spam detection is enabled
- Performance threshold is 2 seconds
- User is logged in

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start timer and submit content | Submission is initiated and timer starts |
| 2 | 2. Wait for submission response | Response is received with publish or spam decision |
| 3 | 3. Stop timer and record duration | Duration is captured |

**Final Expected Result:** Spam detection decision is completed within 2 seconds

---

### TC-564: Masked IP used on successful call connection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Verify that a participant's real IP is not exposed when a call connects.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B through the system | Call setup starts and signaling is established |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connection is established |
| 3 | 3. Inspect call session details/metadata visible to User A | Only a masked IP address is shown for User B |
| 4 | 4. Inspect call session details/metadata visible to User B | Only a masked IP address is shown for User A |

**Final Expected Result:** Both participants see only masked IP addresses; no real IP is exposed.

---

### TC-565: Masked IPs for users on different networks

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Ensure both parties see masked IPs when connected from different networks.

**Preconditions:**
- User A is on Network X
- User B is on Network Y (different public IP range)
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B | Call setup starts and signaling is established |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connection is established |
| 3 | 3. Check IP information displayed to User A | User A sees a masked IP for User B |
| 4 | 4. Check IP information displayed to User B | User B sees a masked IP for User A |

**Final Expected Result:** Masked IPs are displayed to both parties regardless of network location.

---

### TC-566: Block call when masking service is unavailable

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Verify the system blocks the call to prevent exposure of real IP when masking service is down.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B | System detects masking service unavailability |
| 2 | 2. Observe the call outcome | Call is blocked before connection is established |
| 3 | 3. Check system logs | Failure to reach masking service is logged with timestamp and call identifiers |

**Final Expected Result:** Call is blocked and the failure is logged without exposing real IP addresses.

---

### TC-567: Warn user when masking service is unavailable

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Verify user is warned when masking service is down and call is not connected.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B | System detects masking service unavailability |
| 2 | 2. Observe the user-facing message | A warning message indicates IP masking is unavailable and the call cannot proceed |
| 3 | 3. Verify call state | Call does not connect |

**Final Expected Result:** User is warned and the call is prevented from connecting.

---

### TC-568: Negative: real IP not leaked in call logs or UI

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Ensure real IPs are not exposed in any user-facing UI or call logs.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish a call between User A and User B | Call is connected successfully |
| 2 | 2. Review user-facing call details/history for User A | Only masked IP addresses appear; no real IP data |
| 3 | 3. Review user-facing call details/history for User B | Only masked IP addresses appear; no real IP data |

**Final Expected Result:** Real IP addresses are not visible in UI or call history.

---

### TC-569: Boundary: masking service latency within acceptable limits

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Verify call connects with masked IPs when masking service response is slow but available.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service latency is near upper threshold but still responds

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B | Call setup starts and waits for masking service response |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connects after masking service responds |
| 3 | 3. Verify IP information shown to both users | Masked IPs are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Call connects successfully with masked IPs despite high masking service latency.

---

### TC-570: Negative: attempt to bypass masking via direct peer info

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Ensure that direct peer information does not reveal real IPs.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Establish a call between User A and User B | Call is connected successfully |
| 2 | 2. Inspect network connection details visible to User A | Only masked IP endpoints are present |
| 3 | 3. Inspect network connection details visible to User B | Only masked IP endpoints are present |

**Final Expected Result:** No direct peer connection exposes real IPs to either participant.

---

### TC-571: Recovery: masking service restored after failure

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-064
**Requirement:** WA-SEC-008

**Description:** Verify calls can be made after masking service becomes available again.

**Preconditions:**
- User A and User B accounts exist
- Both users are logged in
- Masking service was unavailable and then restored

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. User A initiates a call to User B after service restoration | Call setup proceeds normally |
| 2 | 2. User B accepts the call | Call connects successfully |
| 3 | 3. Verify IP information shown to both users | Masked IPs are displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Calls succeed and masked IPs are shown after service recovery.

---

### TC-572: Push-Benachrichtigung wird bei aktivierten Einstellungen und Online-Gerät pünktlich zugestellt

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Validiert die Zustellung innerhalb der definierten Zustellzeit, wenn Push aktiviert und Gerät online ist

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist online
- Zustellzeit-SLA ist definiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger im System auslösen, der eine relevante Benachrichtigung erzeugt | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Auf dem Gerät die Zeit bis zum Erscheinen der Push-Benachrichtigung messen | Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint auf dem Gerät |

**Final Expected Result:** Push-Benachrichtigung wird innerhalb der definierten Zustellzeit angezeigt

---

### TC-573: Push-Benachrichtigung wird bei Offline-Gerät nach Wiederverbindung zugestellt

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Prüft die automatische Zustellung nach Wiederherstellung der Verbindung

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist offline

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger im System auslösen, der eine relevante Benachrichtigung erzeugt | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Gerät wieder online schalten | Netzwerkverbindung ist wiederhergestellt |
| 3 | 3. Prüfen, ob die Benachrichtigung zugestellt wird | Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint automatisch auf dem Gerät |

**Final Expected Result:** Benachrichtigung wird nach Wiederverbindung automatisch zugestellt

---

### TC-574: Keine Push-Benachrichtigung bei deaktivierten App-Einstellungen

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Validiert, dass bei deaktivierten Push-Einstellungen keine Pushs gesendet werden

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind deaktiviert
- Gerät ist online

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger im System auslösen, der eine relevante Benachrichtigung erzeugt | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Auf dem Gerät auf eingehende Push-Benachrichtigungen prüfen | Keine Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint |

**Final Expected Result:** Es wird keine Push-Benachrichtigung an das Gerät gesendet

---

### TC-575: Grenzfall: Zustellung exakt am Ende der Zustellzeit

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Stellt sicher, dass Zustellung am Grenzwert noch als erfolgreich gilt

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist online
- Zustellzeit-SLA ist definiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger im System auslösen und Zeitstempel erfassen | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Zustellzeit bis zum Erscheinen der Push messen | Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint auf dem Gerät |

**Final Expected Result:** Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint spätestens zum Ende der definierten Zustellzeit

---

### TC-576: Negative: Zustellung überschreitet Zustellzeit

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Validiert Fehlerfall, wenn Zustellzeit überschritten wird

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist online
- Zustellzeit-SLA ist definiert

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger im System auslösen und Zeitstempel erfassen | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Zustellzeit bis zum Erscheinen der Push messen | Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint auf dem Gerät |

**Final Expected Result:** Benachrichtigung wird als verspätet erkannt, wenn die Zustellzeit überschritten wird

---

### TC-577: Offline-Zustellung nur einmal nach Wiederverbindung

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Verhindert Duplikate bei Offline-Queue nach Wiederverbindung

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist offline

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Zwei relevante Benachrichtigungen während Offline-Phase auslösen | Beide Benachrichtigungen werden vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Gerät wieder online schalten | Netzwerkverbindung ist wiederhergestellt |
| 3 | 3. Prüfen, ob jede Benachrichtigung genau einmal ankommt | Jede Benachrichtigung erscheint genau einmal auf dem Gerät |

**Final Expected Result:** Keine Duplikate; alle ausstehenden Benachrichtigungen werden einmal zugestellt

---

### TC-578: Push deaktiviert während Offline-Phase

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Stellt sicher, dass nach Deaktivierung keine Offline-Benachrichtigungen zugestellt werden

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist offline

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Relevante Benachrichtigung während Offline-Phase auslösen | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App deaktivieren | Push-Einstellung ist deaktiviert |
| 3 | 3. Gerät wieder online schalten | Netzwerkverbindung ist wiederhergestellt |

**Final Expected Result:** Keine Push-Benachrichtigung wird nach Wiederverbindung zugestellt

---

### TC-579: Benachrichtigung bei aktivierten Pushs aber OS-Berechtigung verweigert

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Negativtest für verweigerte Systemberechtigung

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- OS-Benachrichtigungsberechtigung ist verweigert
- Gerät ist online

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Relevante Benachrichtigung im System auslösen | Benachrichtigung wird vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Gerät auf eingehende Pushs prüfen | Keine Push-Benachrichtigung erscheint |

**Final Expected Result:** Keine Push-Benachrichtigung wird angezeigt, da OS-Berechtigung fehlt

---

### TC-580: Mehrere Benachrichtigungen in kurzer Zeit

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-065
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-001

**Description:** Prüft Zustellung mehrerer Pushs unter Last

**Preconditions:**
- Nutzer ist eingeloggt
- Push-Benachrichtigungen in der App sind aktiviert
- Gerät ist online

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Mehrere relevante Benachrichtigungen in kurzer Zeit auslösen | Mehrere Benachrichtigungen werden vom Backend erzeugt |
| 2 | 2. Gerät auf eingehende Pushs prüfen | Alle Push-Benachrichtigungen werden angezeigt |

**Final Expected Result:** Alle Benachrichtigungen werden zuverlässig und ohne Ausfall zugestellt

---

### TC-581: Preview resolves placeholders with provided sample data

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-066
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-002

**Description:** Verify preview generation resolves all placeholders using entered sample data

- User is logged in as notification administrator
- Notification template with placeholders is open in configuration view

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enter valid sample data for all placeholders (e.g., {firstName}=Anna, {orderId}=12345) | Sample data fields are populated without validation errors |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Preview' button | Preview generation is triggered |

**Final Expected Result:** Preview displays the template with all placeholders correctly resolved using the provided sample data

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual

**Requirement:** WA-NOT-002

- Notification template with required placeholders is open in configuration view

|---|--------|------------------|

### TC-585: Preview with optional placeholder left empty

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-066
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-002

**Description:** Verify optional placeholder can be empty without blocking preview

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as notification administrator
- Notification template with optional and required placeholders is open in configuration view

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Provide sample data for all required placeholders; leave optional placeholder empty | Only optional placeholder is empty; no validation errors for required fields |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Preview' button | Preview generation is triggered |

**Final Expected Result:** Preview is generated successfully with optional placeholder handled per template rules (e.g., blank or default)

---

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-066
**Requirement:** WA-NOT-002

- Notification template with placeholders is open in configuration view
- Sample data is provided for all placeholders

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select delivery channel 'Email' and click 'Preview' | Email preview is generated |
| 2 | 2. Switch delivery channel to 'SMS' and click 'Preview' | SMS preview is generated |

**Final Expected Result:** Preview reflects the currently selected channel each time with appropriate formatting and constraints
