### TC-771: Verify predefined options list presence and selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Ensure list of predefined backgrounds is shown and selectable.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is in background settings
- Predefined backgrounds are configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open background settings. | List of predefined backgrounds is visible. |
| 2 | 2. Select each available option sequentially. | Each option can be selected and indicates selection state. |

**Final Expected Result:** All predefined backgrounds are displayed and selectable.

---

### TC-772: Persist background after app restart (session boundary)

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-087
**Requirement:** WA-SET-008

**Description:** Verify persistence across app restart or new session without logout.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has saved a non-default background

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Close the app/browser completely. | Application is closed. |
| 2 | 2. Reopen the app/browser and navigate to the same chat. | User is still logged in or logs in successfully. |
| 3 | 3. Observe the chat background. | Previously saved background is applied. |

**Final Expected Result:** Background selection persists across app restarts.

---

### TC-773: Enable Dark Mode from Settings - immediate theme switch

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that selecting Dark Mode in settings switches the UI theme immediately.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Browser supports theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Locate the Dark Mode option in settings | Dark Mode option is visible and selectable |
| 2 | 2. Select/enable the Dark Mode option | UI switches to dark theme immediately (colors, background, text updated) |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark theme is applied instantly after enabling Dark Mode.

---

### TC-774: Persist Dark Mode after logout/login on same device

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that Dark Mode preference is retained after logout and login on the same device.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Dark Mode is enabled
- Browser supports theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out of the application | User is logged out and redirected to login page |
| 2 | 2. Log back in with the same user on the same device | User is logged in successfully |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to any page after login | Dark theme is still applied across the interface |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark Mode remains enabled after re-login on the same device.

---

### TC-775: Attempt to enable Dark Mode on unsupported browser

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that the app shows a not-supported message and retains current theme when theme switching is unsupported.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Browser does not support theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select/enable the Dark Mode option | A message indicates Dark Mode is not supported |
| 2 | 2. Observe the interface theme | Current theme remains unchanged |

**Final Expected Result:** App displays not-supported message and keeps the existing theme.

---

### TC-776: Disable Dark Mode - switch back to Light Mode

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that disabling Dark Mode switches the UI back to light theme immediately.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Dark Mode is enabled
- Browser supports theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Locate the Dark Mode option in settings | Dark Mode option is visible and currently enabled |
| 2 | 2. Deselect/disable the Dark Mode option | UI switches to light theme immediately |

**Final Expected Result:** Light theme is applied instantly after disabling Dark Mode.

---

### TC-777: Dark Mode preference stored per device (boundary: different device)

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that Dark Mode preference is not incorrectly applied on a different device when only same-device retention is expected.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in on Device A
- Dark Mode is enabled on Device A
- Browser supports theme switching on both devices

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Log out on Device A | User is logged out on Device A |
| 2 | 2. Log in on Device B with the same user account | User is logged in on Device B |
| 3 | 3. Observe theme on Device B | Theme follows default setting for Device B unless explicitly enabled |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark Mode is not automatically applied on a different device unless enabled there.

---

### TC-778: Immediate theme switch across settings and main UI

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that the theme change applies consistently across settings and main UI pages.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Browser supports theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enable Dark Mode in settings | Settings page theme switches to dark immediately |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to a main application page | Main application page is displayed in dark theme |

**Final Expected Result:** Dark theme applies consistently across all pages after enabling.

---

### TC-779: Negative: Rapid toggle of Dark Mode

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify that rapid toggling does not cause UI inconsistencies or errors (boundary condition).

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Browser supports theme switching

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Toggle Dark Mode on and off rapidly 5 times | No errors occur and the toggle responds each time |
| 2 | 2. Stop toggling with Dark Mode enabled | UI settles in dark theme without visual glitches |

**Final Expected Result:** Final theme matches last toggle state and UI remains stable.

---

### TC-780: Negative: Attempt Dark Mode with blocked local storage

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-088
**Requirement:** WA-SET-009

**Description:** Verify behavior when preference storage is blocked and persistence cannot be saved.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is viewing the application settings
- Browser supports theme switching
- Local storage/cookies are blocked

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enable Dark Mode | UI switches to dark theme immediately |
| 2 | 2. Log out and log back in on the same device | Dark Mode may not persist due to blocked storage |

**Final Expected Result:** UI switches to dark mode immediately but persistence is not guaranteed when storage is blocked.

---

### TC-781: Select supported language on current page updates all interface text

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that selecting a supported language changes the interface text on the current page.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured (e.g., English, German, French)
- Visitor is on a page with visible interface text

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open the language selector on the current page. | A list of supported languages is displayed. |
| 2 | Select a supported language (e.g., German). | All visible interface text on the current page is displayed in German. |

**Final Expected Result:** The current page is fully localized to the selected supported language.

---

### TC-782: Language preference persists across navigation within session

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that language selection persists when navigating to another page.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Visitor has selected a supported language (e.g., German)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Navigate to a different page using in-app navigation. | The destination page loads and all interface text is displayed in the previously selected language. |

**Final Expected Result:** Language preference is retained across navigation within the same session.

---

### TC-783: Language preference persists after page refresh in session

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that language selection persists after refreshing the page.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Visitor has selected a supported language (e.g., French)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Refresh the current page using browser refresh. | The page reloads and all interface text remains in the selected language. |

**Final Expected Result:** Language preference is preserved after refresh within the same session.

---

### TC-784: Selecting unsupported language shows error and keeps current language

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify the system prevents switching to an unsupported language and shows a message.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Current language is English
- A non-supported language option is attempted (e.g., via URL parameter or forced selection)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Attempt to select a language not supported by the system. | A message is displayed indicating the language is not available. |
| 2 | Observe the interface text after the attempt. | The interface remains in the current language (English). |

**Final Expected Result:** Unsupported language selection is rejected with a message and no language change.

---

### TC-785: Language selection reflects across subsequent navigation and UI elements

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that all UI elements and system messages are localized after language selection.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Visitor is on a page with navigation menu and system messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Select a supported language (e.g., French) from the language selector. | All interface text, including menu labels and buttons, appears in French on the current page. |
| 2 | Trigger a standard system message (e.g., success toast). | The system message text is displayed in French. |
| 3 | Navigate to another page. | All interface text on the new page appears in French. |

**Final Expected Result:** All interface elements and messages are localized consistently across pages.

---

### TC-786: Boundary: Single language configured hides or disables language selection

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify behavior when only one language is configured.

**Preconditions:**
- Only one language is configured in the system

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open the page header or settings where language selection is typically available. | Language selector is hidden or disabled, showing only the configured language. |

**Final Expected Result:** No language switching is possible when only one language is configured.

---

### TC-787: Boundary: Switching back to original language

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that switching to another supported language and back restores original language correctly.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Current language is English

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Select a supported language different from English (e.g., German). | Interface text is displayed in German. |
| 2 | Select English again from the language selector. | Interface text is displayed in English. |

**Final Expected Result:** Language switching is reversible and accurately reflects the chosen language.

---

### TC-788: Invalid language selection via URL parameter

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that an invalid language passed via URL does not change the interface language.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Current language is English

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | Open a page with an invalid language parameter in the URL (e.g., ?lang=xx). | A message indicates the language is not available or the system ignores the invalid parameter. |
| 2 | Observe the interface language after the page loads. | Interface remains in English. |

**Final Expected Result:** Invalid language parameters do not affect the current language and show appropriate feedback.

---

### TC-789: Session persistence resets after new session

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-089
**Requirement:** WA-SET-010

**Description:** Verify that language preference is not persisted across new sessions if only session storage is used.

**Preconditions:**
- Multiple languages are configured
- Visitor selected a supported language in a previous session

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | End the current browser session (close all browser windows). | Session is terminated. |
| 2 | Open the site in a new session. | The interface displays the default language. |

**Final Expected Result:** Language preference is stored only for the active session and resets on a new session.

---

### TC-790: Initiate cloud backup successfully with active internet

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify authenticated user can back up chat history to cloud and receives completion confirmation

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has existing chat history
- Device has active internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts and progress indicator is shown |
| 3 | 3. Wait for backup to complete | Success message confirms backup completion and timestamp is updated |

**Final Expected Result:** Chat history is successfully backed up to the cloud and user is notified of completion

---

### TC-791: Backup is up to date when no new messages exist

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify backup completes quickly and indicates no changes since last backup

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A successful backup was completed recently
- No new messages since last backup
- Device has active internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed with last backup timestamp |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts |
| 3 | 3. Observe backup completion | Backup completes quickly and message indicates 'Backup is up to date' |

**Final Expected Result:** System identifies no changes and confirms backup is current

---

### TC-792: Backup fails gracefully when internet is unavailable

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify system handles offline backup initiation with a clear error and guidance

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Device has no internet connection
- User has existing chat history

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | System detects no internet and backup does not start |
| 3 | 3. Review error message | Error message indicates no connection and suggests retrying when online |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup fails gracefully with actionable guidance to retry

---

### TC-793: Retry backup after restoring internet connection

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify user can retry and complete backup after initial failure due to connectivity loss

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Backup previously failed due to no internet
- Internet connection is restored

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Return to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts successfully |
| 3 | 3. Wait for backup to complete | Success message confirms backup completion |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup completes successfully after retry when internet is available

---

### TC-794: Backup initiation blocked for unauthenticated user

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify unauthenticated users cannot start a cloud backup

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged out
- Device has active internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | User is prompted to log in or backup option is disabled |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to initiate backup | System blocks action and requests authentication |

**Final Expected Result:** Cloud backup cannot be initiated without authentication

---

### TC-795: Backup completes when chat history is large

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify backup handles large chat history without failure

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has large chat history (e.g., >10,000 messages)
- Device has active internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts and progress indicator is shown |
| 3 | 3. Monitor completion time and outcome | Backup completes successfully within acceptable time and confirms completion |

**Final Expected Result:** Large chat history is backed up successfully without errors

---

### TC-796: Interrupt internet connection during backup

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify backup fails gracefully if connectivity is lost mid-process

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has existing chat history
- Device has active internet connection

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup | Backup screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts |
| 3 | 3. Disable internet connection during backup | Backup stops and system detects connection loss |
| 4 | 4. Review error message | Error displayed with guidance to retry when online |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup fails gracefully and user is instructed to retry

---

### TC-797: Backup status timestamp updates after successful backup

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-090
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-001

**Description:** Verify last backup timestamp updates on completion

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Device has active internet connection
- User has existing chat history

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Settings > Backup and note last backup timestamp | Existing timestamp is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Tap 'Start Cloud Backup' | Backup process starts |
| 3 | 3. Wait for backup to complete | Success message appears |
| 4 | 4. Review last backup timestamp | Timestamp updates to current time |

**Final Expected Result:** Last backup timestamp reflects the most recent successful backup

---

### TC-798: Successful end-to-end encrypted backup with valid key

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify backup completes and is stored encrypted when a valid encryption key is available.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- Administrator has access to backup settings
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- A valid encryption key is configured and available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Backup Settings | Backup settings page is displayed with encryption status shown |
| 2 | 2. Confirm the valid encryption key is selected/active | Valid key is indicated as active |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Start Backup' | Backup process starts and progress indicator appears |
| 4 | 4. Wait for completion | System shows success confirmation message |
| 5 | 5. Verify stored backup metadata | Backup record indicates 'Encrypted: Yes' and encryption metadata present |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup completes successfully and is stored encrypted end-to-end.

---

### TC-799: Exported encrypted backup remains unreadable without key

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify exported backup remains encrypted and cannot be read without correct key.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- A recent encrypted backup exists
- External storage location is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Backup History | List of backups is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an encrypted backup and click 'Export' | Export process starts and completes successfully |
| 3 | 3. Transfer exported backup to external storage | Backup file appears in external storage |
| 4 | 4. Attempt to open/read the backup file without providing a decryption key | File content is unreadable or access is denied |

**Final Expected Result:** Exported backup remains encrypted and unreadable without the correct key.

---

### TC-800: Attempt to start backup with no encryption key

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify backup is prevented and error is shown when no encryption key is provided.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- Administrator has access to backup settings
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- No encryption key is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Backup Settings | Backup settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Start Backup' | System blocks backup initiation |
| 3 | 3. Observe system message | Clear error message indicates missing encryption key |
| 4 | 4. Check audit/logs for backup attempt | Failure is logged with reason 'missing key' |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup does not start; user sees error message and failure is logged.

---

### TC-801: Attempt to start backup with invalid encryption key

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify backup is prevented and error is shown when an invalid key is provided.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- Administrator has access to backup settings
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- An invalid or corrupted encryption key is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Backup Settings | Backup settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Confirm the invalid key is selected/active | Invalid key appears active |
| 3 | 3. Click 'Start Backup' | System blocks backup initiation |
| 4 | 4. Observe system message | Clear error message indicates invalid encryption key |
| 5 | 5. Check audit/logs for backup attempt | Failure is logged with reason 'invalid key' |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup does not start; user sees error message and failure is logged.

---

### TC-802: Boundary test: Encryption key just expired at backup time

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify backup is blocked when the key is expired at initiation time.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- Administrator has access to backup settings
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- Encryption key expires at current time

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Backup Settings | Backup settings page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click 'Start Backup' immediately after key expiry | System validates key status |
| 3 | 3. Observe system response | Backup is blocked and an error message indicates key expired |
| 4 | 4. Check audit/logs for backup attempt | Failure is logged with reason 'key expired' |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup is prevented with clear error; failure is logged.

---

### TC-803: Verify encryption status in backup metadata after successful backup

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Ensure encryption metadata is correctly recorded for compliant auditing.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- Valid encryption key is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start a backup | Backup starts and completes successfully |
| 2 | 2. Open backup details/metadata | Backup details are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Verify encryption fields | Fields show encryption enabled, algorithm, and key ID (not key material) |

**Final Expected Result:** Backup metadata reflects end-to-end encryption without exposing key material.

---

### TC-804: Export encrypted backup and verify decryption succeeds with correct key

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Confirm that the encrypted backup can be decrypted only with the correct key.

**Preconditions:**
- Administrator is logged in
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- A recent encrypted backup exists
- Correct decryption key is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Export an encrypted backup | Export completes successfully |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to decrypt the backup with the correct key | Decryption process starts |
| 3 | 3. Verify decrypted content integrity | Decrypted backup is readable and passes integrity checks |

**Final Expected Result:** Only the correct key allows successful decryption and integrity validation.

---

### TC-805: Unauthorized user cannot initiate encrypted backup

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-091
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-002

**Description:** Verify access control for backup initiation.

**Preconditions:**
- Non-admin user is logged in
- End-to-end encryption is enabled
- Valid encryption key is configured

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to access Backup Settings | Access is denied or settings are read-only |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to start a backup | System blocks action and shows permission error |

**Final Expected Result:** Non-admin users cannot initiate backups.

---

### TC-806: Export single chat successfully

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify that an authorized user can export a selected chat and receive a downloadable file

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to Chat A
- Chat A contains messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Chat A | Chat A conversation view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | Export dialog/options are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Select the default export format and confirm export | System generates export and prompts a file download |
| 4 | 4. Download and open the exported file | Exported file opens without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** A downloadable export of the selected chat is provided and is readable

---

### TC-807: Export includes all messages and metadata

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify export includes all messages and associated metadata in the chosen format

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to Chat B
- Chat B contains multiple messages with timestamps, sender info, and attachments

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Chat B | Chat B conversation view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | Export dialog/options are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Choose the supported export format (e.g., PDF/JSON) and confirm | System generates export and prompts a file download |
| 4 | 4. Open the exported file and verify content | All messages, timestamps, sender metadata, and attachment references are present |

**Final Expected Result:** Export contains all messages and associated metadata in the chosen format

---

### TC-808: Unauthorized user cannot export chat

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify export is denied when user lacks permission

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User does not have access to Chat C

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to access export via direct URL or UI for Chat C | System prevents access to export action |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option if visible | System denies the export request |

**Final Expected Result:** An authorization error is displayed and no file is downloaded

---

### TC-809: Export chat with attachments

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify attachments are included or referenced according to export format

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User has access to Chat D
- Chat D contains attachments of multiple types

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Chat D | Chat D conversation view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | Export dialog/options are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Select a format that supports attachments and confirm export | System generates export and prompts a file download |
| 4 | 4. Open the exported file and inspect attachment data | Attachments are included or properly referenced per format specification |

**Final Expected Result:** Export includes attachments or valid references without missing items

---

### TC-810: Export empty chat

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify export behavior when a chat contains no messages

- User is logged in
- User has access to Chat E
- Chat E contains no messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Chat E | Chat E conversation view is displayed with no messages |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | Export dialog/options are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm export | System generates export and prompts a file download |
| 4 | 4. Open the exported file | Export file indicates no messages without errors |

**Final Expected Result:** Export completes successfully with an empty conversation representation

### TC-811: Export large chat conversation

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify export completes for a chat with a large number of messages

- User has access to Chat F
- Chat F contains a large number of messages (e.g., 10,000+)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to Chat F | Chat F conversation view is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | Export dialog/options are displayed |
| 3 | 3. Confirm export and monitor processing | System processes the export without timeout or failure |
| 4 | 4. Download and open the exported file | Exported file is complete and opens successfully |

**Final Expected Result:** Export completes within acceptable time and includes all messages

### TC-812: Export with invalid session

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-092
**Requirement:** WA-BAK-003

**Description:** Verify export is blocked when session is expired

**Preconditions:**
- User session has expired
- Chat G exists and user had prior access

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to export Chat G | System detects expired session |
| 2 | 2. Click the 'Export chat' option | System prompts for re-authentication or shows session expired error |

**Final Expected Result:** Export is not allowed until user re-authenticates
