### TC-911: View basic message statistics for valid time range

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify that a business admin can view total, sent, and received messages for a selected time range with existing data

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as business account admin
- User has access to a business with existing messages
- Statistics section is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Business Statistics section | Business Statistics section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid time range that contains messages (e.g., last 7 days) | Selected time range is applied |
| 3 | 3. Request statistics for the selected time range | System displays total messages and sent vs. received counts for the range |

**Final Expected Result:** Statistics are displayed accurately for the selected time range

---

### TC-912: Display zero values when no messages in time range

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify that the system shows zero counts and a no-data indication for a time range with no messages

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as business account admin
- User has access to a business
- Selected time range has no messages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Business Statistics section | Business Statistics section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a time range with no messages | Selected time range is applied |
| 3 | 3. Request statistics for the selected time range | System displays zero values for total, sent, and received |
| 4 | 4. Observe the data availability indicator | System indicates that no data is available for the selected period |

**Final Expected Result:** Zero values and no-data indicator are shown for the selected period

---

### TC-913: Deny access for unauthorized user

**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify that a user without business access cannot view statistics

|---|--------|------------------|

### TC-914: Boundary: Single-day time range with messages

**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

| 3 | 3. Request statistics for the selected day | System displays total, sent, and received counts for that day |

**Final Expected Result:** Statistics are accurate for the single-day range

### TC-915: Boundary: Time range start equals end with no messages

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify zero values and no-data indication for a single-day range with no messages

- User has access to a business
- There are no messages on the selected day

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|

| 2 | 2. Select a single-day time range with no messages | Single-day range is applied |
| 3 | 3. Request statistics for the selected day | System displays zero values and indicates no data |

### TC-916: Validation: Invalid time range (start date after end date)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify the system validates invalid time ranges and prevents statistics request

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as business account admin
- User has access to a business

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Business Statistics section | Business Statistics section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select an invalid time range where start date is after end date | System shows validation error or disables request |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to request statistics | Request is blocked and error message is displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Invalid time ranges are rejected with a clear error message

### TC-917: Consistency: Sent + received equals total

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify the total messages count equals the sum of sent and received

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as business account admin
- User has access to a business with messages in selected range

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Business Statistics section | Business Statistics section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a valid time range with messages | Selected time range is applied |
| 3 | 3. Request statistics and capture total, sent, and received values | Statistics values are displayed |
| 4 | 4. Compare total to the sum of sent and received | Total equals sent plus received |

**Final Expected Result:** Total messages equals the sum of sent and received values

---

### TC-918: Performance: Statistics response time

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-104
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-009

**Description:** Verify statistics load within acceptable response time for a large date range

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in as business account admin
- User has access to a business with large message volume
- Performance threshold is defined (e.g., < 3 seconds)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the Business Statistics section | Business Statistics section is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a large time range (e.g., last 12 months) | Selected time range is applied |
| 3 | 3. Request statistics and measure response time | System returns statistics within the defined threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** Statistics are displayed within acceptable response time

---

### TC-919: Successful API access with valid credentials and valid request

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify authorized endpoint returns HTTP 200 and expected payload when credentials and parameters are valid

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint is available
- Test data exists for the request

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials (e.g., API key or OAuth token) | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request to authorized API endpoint with all required parameters valid | Request is accepted by API gateway |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 200 and response body matches expected schema and values |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 200 with expected response payload

---

### TC-920: Missing required parameter returns 400

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API returns HTTP 400 and clear error message when required parameter is missing

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request to endpoint omitting a required parameter | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 400 and response contains a clear error message identifying the missing parameter |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 400 with clear validation error message

---

### TC-921: Invalid parameter type returns 400

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API validates parameter types and returns 400 with clear error message

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with a parameter of invalid type (e.g., string instead of integer) | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 400 and response contains a clear error message indicating invalid parameter type |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 400 with clear error message for invalid parameter type

---

### TC-922: Invalid parameter value boundary returns 400

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API enforces boundary constraints and returns 400 for out-of-range values

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint is available
- Boundary rules are defined for the parameter

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with parameter value outside allowed range | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 400 and response contains a clear error message indicating the boundary violation |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 400 with clear boundary validation error message

---

### TC-923: Invalid credentials return 401

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API returns 401 and does not process request when credentials are invalid

**Preconditions:**
- Authorized API endpoint is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with invalid API credentials | Invalid credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with otherwise valid parameters | Request is received by API gateway |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 401 and response indicates unauthorized access |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 401 and request is not processed

---

### TC-924: Expired credentials return 401

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API returns 401 and does not process request when credentials are expired

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has expired API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with expired API credentials | Expired credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with valid parameters | Request is received by API gateway |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 401 and response indicates credentials are expired or unauthorized |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 401 and request is not processed

---

### TC-925: No credentials provided returns 401

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API returns 401 when no credentials are supplied

**Preconditions:**
- Authorized API endpoint is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request without any API credentials | No authentication headers are present |
| 2 | 2. Send request with valid parameters | Request is received by API gateway |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 401 and response indicates unauthorized access |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 401 and request is not processed

---

### TC-926: Valid credentials with invalid payload format returns 400

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API returns 400 when payload format is invalid (e.g., malformed JSON)

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint accepts JSON payload

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with malformed JSON body | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 400 and response contains a clear error message about invalid payload format |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 400 with clear error message for invalid payload format

---

### TC-927: Valid credentials with optional parameter omitted returns 200

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API handles optional parameters correctly and still returns 200

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint has optional parameters

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with only required parameters, omitting optional parameters | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 200 and response body matches expected schema and values |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 200 when optional parameters are omitted

---

### TC-928: Valid credentials with boundary max value returns 200

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-105
**Requirement:** WA-BUS-010

**Description:** Verify API accepts maximum boundary values and returns 200

**Preconditions:**
- Integration partner has valid API credentials
- Authorized API endpoint has defined max boundary for a parameter

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure request with valid API credentials | Credentials are included in request headers |
| 2 | 2. Send request with parameter set to maximum allowed value | Request is received by API |
| 3 | 3. Capture the response status and body | HTTP status is 200 and response body matches expected schema and values |

**Final Expected Result:** API returns HTTP 200 for maximum boundary value

---

### TC-929: Announce all interactive elements with correct labels, roles, and states

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify screen reader announces labels, roles, and states for interactive elements in logical order

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with common interactive elements (buttons, links, toggles, menus)
- Screen reader is enabled
- Keyboard navigation is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Use Tab/Shift+Tab to navigate through all interactive elements | Focus moves in a logical order matching visual layout |
| 2 | 2. Listen to each screen reader announcement as focus changes | Each element is announced with correct accessible name, role, and state (e.g., button, link, expanded/collapsed, checked/unchecked) |
| 3 | 3. Activate a toggle or checkbox using keyboard | Screen reader announces updated state (e.g., checked/unchecked) |

**Final Expected Result:** All interactive elements are announced with correct labels, roles, and states in a logical order

---

### TC-930: Detect missing or incorrect accessible labels on interactive elements (negative)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Ensure elements without accessible labels are detected by screen reader behavior

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page containing at least one icon-only button or link
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to the icon-only button using Tab | Focus lands on the button |
| 2 | 2. Listen to the screen reader announcement | If label is missing, the screen reader announces a generic name (e.g., 'button') or unreadable name |

**Final Expected Result:** Unlabeled interactive elements are detectable and can be reported for remediation

---

### TC-931: Announce form control purpose, required status, and current value

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify screen reader reads purpose, required status, and current value for focused form controls

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a form page with required and optional fields
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Focus a required text input using Tab | Screen reader announces label and required status |
| 2 | 2. Enter a value into the required input | Screen reader announces current value when focus returns to the field |
| 3 | 3. Focus an optional select/dropdown control | Screen reader announces label, role (combobox/select), and current selection |
| 4 | 4. Focus a radio group and move between options | Screen reader announces group label, selected state, and option labels |

**Final Expected Result:** Focused controls announce purpose, required status, and current value accurately

---

### TC-932: Announce dynamic content updates via live regions

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify screen reader announces content updates without full page reload using live region semantics

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with dynamic content updates (e.g., toast messages, search results, cart updates)
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger an action that updates content dynamically (e.g., add item to cart) | Dynamic content updates without a page reload |
| 2 | 2. Listen for screen reader announcement | Screen reader announces the update via live region (e.g., 'Item added to cart') |
| 3 | 3. Trigger another update quickly (e.g., add a second item) | Screen reader announces the new update without missing or overlapping messages |

**Final Expected Result:** Dynamic updates are announced using appropriate live region semantics

---

### TC-933: No live region announcement for silent updates (negative)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Validate that updates intended to be silent do not produce screen reader announcements

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with non-critical visual updates
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger a non-critical UI update (e.g., loading spinner appears) | UI updates visually without a full page reload |
| 2 | 2. Listen for screen reader announcements | No announcement is made for the silent update |

**Final Expected Result:** Non-critical updates do not generate unnecessary screen reader announcements

---

### TC-934: Announce validation errors and focus first invalid field

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify screen reader announces errors on form submission and focuses first invalid field

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a form with required fields
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave multiple required fields empty | Fields remain empty with no errors yet |
| 2 | 2. Submit the form using keyboard | Validation errors appear without page reload |
| 3 | 3. Listen to screen reader output and observe focus | Screen reader announces error summary and focus moves to the first invalid field |
| 4 | 4. Move focus away and back to the invalid field | Screen reader announces the specific error message for the field |

**Final Expected Result:** Errors are announced and focus moves to the first invalid field

---

### TC-935: Single-field error announcement boundary case

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify error announcement when only one field is invalid

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a form with one required field
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Leave the only required field empty | No error is shown prior to submission |
| 2 | 2. Submit the form | Validation error appears for the field |
| 3 | 3. Listen to screen reader output | Screen reader announces the error and focuses the invalid field |

**Final Expected Result:** Single-field validation error is announced and focus is correctly set

---

### TC-936: Screen reader announces current state of expandable menu items

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify state changes for expandable/collapsible elements are announced

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with expandable menu or accordion
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a collapsed menu item using keyboard | Screen reader announces the item and its collapsed state |
| 2 | 2. Activate the item to expand | Screen reader announces expanded state and any revealed content |
| 3 | 3. Activate again to collapse | Screen reader announces collapsed state |

**Final Expected Result:** Expandable elements announce state changes correctly

---

### TC-937: Logical focus order across complex layout

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Ensure keyboard focus order matches visual reading order across columns and sections

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with multi-column layout and multiple sections
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start at the top of the page and use Tab to navigate | Focus moves top-to-bottom and left-to-right as visually presented |
| 2 | 2. Navigate through all sections | No unexpected jumps or skipped elements in focus order |

**Final Expected Result:** Focus order is logical and matches visual layout

---

### TC-938: Screen reader announces current value for range/slider control (boundary values)

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-106
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-001

**Description:** Verify slider control announces min/max/current values at boundaries

**Preconditions:**
- User is on a page with a range/slider input
- Screen reader is enabled

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Focus the slider using keyboard | Screen reader announces label, role, and current value |
| 2 | 2. Move slider to minimum value using keyboard | Screen reader announces updated current value at minimum |
| 3 | 3. Move slider to maximum value using keyboard | Screen reader announces updated current value at maximum |

**Final Expected Result:** Slider control announces current value accurately at boundary values

---

### TC-939: Update font size on current page immediately

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify that selecting a different font size updates text on the current page instantly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on a content page with text
- Font size settings are accessible

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open font size settings | Font size options are displayed |
| 2 | 2. Select a larger font size option | Text on the current page updates immediately to the selected size |

**Final Expected Result:** The current page text updates instantly to the chosen font size

---

### TC-940: Persist font size after navigation to another page

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify that the selected font size persists when navigating to another page

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on a content page with text

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open font size settings and select a smaller font size | Text on the current page updates to the smaller size |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to a different content page | Text on the new page is displayed using the previously selected font size |

**Final Expected Result:** Chosen font size is applied consistently across pages

---

### TC-941: Persist font size after logout and login

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify that the font size setting is saved and applied after re-login

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on a content page with text

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select a different font size from settings | Text on the current page updates to the selected size |
| 2 | 2. Log out of the application | User is logged out successfully |
| 3 | 3. Log in again and open any content page | Text is displayed using the previously selected font size |

**Final Expected Result:** Font size preference is persisted across sessions

---

### TC-942: Enforce minimum font size boundary

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify the system prevents selecting a font size below the minimum limit

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Minimum and maximum font size limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to select a font size smaller than the minimum limit | System prevents the selection |
| 2 | 2. Observe the applied font size | Font size remains at the minimum allowed value |

**Final Expected Result:** Selection below minimum is blocked and nearest allowed size is kept

---

### TC-943: Enforce maximum font size boundary

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify the system prevents selecting a font size above the maximum limit

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Minimum and maximum font size limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Attempt to select a font size larger than the maximum limit | System prevents the selection |
| 2 | 2. Observe the applied font size | Font size remains at the maximum allowed value |

**Final Expected Result:** Selection above maximum is blocked and nearest allowed size is kept

---

### TC-944: Select minimum font size within allowed range

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify selecting the minimum allowed size applies correctly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Minimum and maximum font size limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open font size settings and select the minimum allowed size | Text on the current page updates to the minimum size |

**Final Expected Result:** Minimum allowed font size is applied successfully

---

### TC-945: Select maximum font size within allowed range

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify selecting the maximum allowed size applies correctly

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- Minimum and maximum font size limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open font size settings and select the maximum allowed size | Text on the current page updates to the maximum size |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum allowed font size is applied successfully

---

### TC-946: No change when selecting current font size

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify that selecting the currently active font size does not cause unintended changes

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on a content page with text

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Note the current font size setting | Current font size is identified |
| 2 | 2. Select the same font size option | No visual change occurs and font size remains consistent |

**Final Expected Result:** Re-selecting the same size does not alter content display

---

### TC-947: Apply font size consistently after browser refresh

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-107
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-002

**Description:** Verify that the font size remains applied after refreshing the page

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- User is on a content page with text

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select a different font size from settings | Text updates to the selected size |
| 2 | 2. Refresh the browser page | Text remains at the selected font size |

**Final Expected Result:** Font size preference persists on page refresh

---

### TC-948: Verify WCAG AA contrast for standard theme text and icons

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Ensure foreground/background contrast meets WCAG AA in standard theme for normal text, large text, and icons.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- User can access public pages

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the homepage in standard theme | Homepage loads with standard theme styles applied |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for normal body text against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 |
| 3 | 3. Measure contrast ratio for large text (≥18pt or 14pt bold) against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 3:1 |
| 4 | 4. Measure contrast ratio for icons used for navigation and actions against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 3:1 |

**Final Expected Result:** All standard theme text and icons meet or exceed WCAG AA contrast ratios.

---

### TC-949: Verify contrast for interactive components across states in standard theme

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Check buttons, links, form labels, and status indicators maintain sufficient contrast in all states.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- User can access pages with interactive components

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a page containing buttons, links, and form controls | Interactive components are visible on the page |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for default button text/icon against button background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 3:1 for UI component text/icons |
| 3 | 3. Hover over buttons and links and measure contrast ratios | Hover state contrast ratios meet ≥ 3:1 for UI components and ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text |
| 4 | 4. Focus each interactive element using keyboard navigation (Tab) | Focus indicators and focused text/icons meet required contrast ratios |
| 5 | 5. Activate (click) elements and measure active state contrast | Active state contrast ratios meet required thresholds |
| 6 | 6. Verify disabled state components and measure contrast | Disabled state still meets contrast requirements for UI components where applicable |

**Final Expected Result:** All interactive components meet WCAG AA contrast requirements in all states.

---

### TC-950: Verify contrast for form labels and error/status indicators

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Ensure labels and status indicators meet contrast requirements in standard theme.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- Form with validation exists

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open a page with a form containing labels and helper text | Form labels and helper text are visible |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for form labels against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text |
| 3 | 3. Trigger a validation error and measure error message contrast | Error message contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 |
| 4 | 4. Trigger a success/status indicator and measure contrast | Status indicator text/icon meets ≥ 3:1 for UI components and ≥ 4.5:1 for text |

**Final Expected Result:** Form labels and status indicators meet WCAG AA contrast ratios.

---

### TC-951: Verify contrast in high-contrast/dark mode

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Ensure alternate theme maintains required contrast ratios for text and UI components.

**Preconditions:**
- High-contrast or dark mode is available
- User can access theme settings

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Enable high-contrast or dark mode from settings | Interface switches to alternate theme |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for normal text across multiple sections | Contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 |
| 3 | 3. Measure contrast ratio for large text and UI components (buttons, icons) | Contrast ratio is ≥ 3:1 |
| 4 | 4. Check interactive elements in hover, focus, active, and disabled states | All states meet required contrast thresholds |

**Final Expected Result:** Alternate theme maintains WCAG AA contrast compliance across all components.

---

### TC-952: Negative test: Detect insufficient contrast in custom content block

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Validate that a low-contrast custom content block is flagged as non-compliant.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- Page contains a configurable content block

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Configure a content block with light gray text on white background | Content block renders with the chosen colors |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for the block text | Contrast ratio is < 4.5:1 |
| 3 | 3. Run accessibility audit or validation tool | Audit flags the block as failing WCAG AA contrast requirements |

**Final Expected Result:** System identifies and reports insufficient contrast in the custom block.

---

### TC-953: Boundary test: Contrast ratio exactly at minimum thresholds

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Ensure colors at boundary thresholds are accepted as compliant.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- Test page allows custom theme overrides

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Set text and background colors to achieve contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text | Text renders with the configured colors |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for normal text | Measured ratio is exactly 4.5:1 |
| 3 | 3. Set large text to achieve contrast ratio of 3:1 and render on page | Large text renders with configured colors |
| 4 | 4. Measure contrast ratio for large text | Measured ratio is exactly 3:1 |

**Final Expected Result:** Boundary contrast ratios are accepted as compliant.

---

### TC-954: Verify contrast on navigation menu and footer links

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Ensure navigation and footer links meet contrast requirements across sections.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- Site has navigation and footer

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the homepage and view the top navigation menu | Navigation links are visible |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for navigation link text against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 |
| 3 | 3. Scroll to footer and measure contrast ratio for footer links | Footer links meet ≥ 4.5:1 contrast ratio |

**Final Expected Result:** Navigation and footer links meet WCAG AA contrast requirements.

---

### TC-955: Verify contrast on cards and section headings across pages

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-108
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-003

**Description:** Validate contrast for text on cards and section headings in different sections.

**Preconditions:**
- Standard theme is active
- Multiple sections with cards exist

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a page containing content cards | Cards and section headings are visible |
| 2 | 2. Measure contrast ratio for card titles and body text against card background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text and ≥ 3:1 for large text |
| 3 | 3. Measure contrast ratio for section headings against background | Contrast ratio is ≥ 3:1 for large text or ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text |

**Final Expected Result:** Cards and section headings meet required contrast ratios.

---

### TC-956: Transcribe received voice message (supported language, good quality)

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription is generated for a received voice message with supported language and acceptable audio quality.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A received voice message exists in a supported language
- Voice message audio quality is acceptable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the received voice message | The voice message is visible with available actions |
| 2 | 2. Select the transcription option for the voice message | A transcription request is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Wait for transcription to complete | Readable transcription text is displayed alongside the voice message with minimal delay |

**Final Expected Result:** The system provides a readable transcription displayed next to the voice message.

---

### TC-957: Transcribe sent voice message (supported language, good quality)

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription is generated for a sent voice message with supported language and acceptable audio quality.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A sent voice message exists in a supported language
- Voice message audio quality is acceptable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the sent voice message | The voice message is visible with available actions |
| 2 | 2. Select the transcription option for the voice message | A transcription request is initiated |
| 3 | 3. Wait for transcription to complete | Readable transcription text is displayed alongside the voice message with minimal delay |

**Final Expected Result:** The system provides a readable transcription displayed next to the voice message.

---

### TC-958: Unsupported language handling

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify the system informs the user when transcription is requested for an unsupported language.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists in an unsupported language

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the voice message | The voice message is visible with available actions |
| 2 | 2. Select the transcription option for the voice message | The system evaluates language support |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | User is informed that transcription is not possible or may be inaccurate |

**Final Expected Result:** The system displays a clear message indicating unsupported language transcription limitations.

---

### TC-959: Poor audio quality handling

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify the system informs the user when transcription is requested for poor audio quality.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists with poor audio quality in a supported language

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Open the chat containing the voice message | The voice message is visible with available actions |
| 2 | 2. Select the transcription option for the voice message | The system evaluates audio quality |
| 3 | 3. Observe the system response | User is informed that transcription is not possible or may be inaccurate |

**Final Expected Result:** The system displays a clear warning about poor audio quality affecting transcription.

---

### TC-960: Minimal delay for supported transcription

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription is generated with minimal delay for supported language and acceptable audio quality.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists in a supported language with acceptable audio quality
- Performance baseline and acceptable delay threshold are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the transcription option for the voice message | Transcription request is initiated |
| 2 | 2. Measure time until transcription appears | Transcription is displayed within the acceptable delay threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** Transcription appears within the defined minimal delay.

---

### TC-961: Transcription displayed alongside message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription text is displayed adjacent to the corresponding voice message.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists in a supported language with acceptable audio quality

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Request transcription for the voice message | Transcription request is initiated |
| 2 | 2. Observe the UI after transcription completes | Transcription text appears next to the correct voice message |

**Final Expected Result:** Transcription is correctly associated with and displayed alongside the voice message.

---

### TC-962: Boundary: very short voice message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription works for a very short voice message (e.g., 1–2 seconds).

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A very short voice message exists in a supported language with acceptable audio quality

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the transcription option for the short voice message | Transcription request is initiated |
| 2 | 2. Wait for transcription to complete | A readable transcription is generated and displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** Short voice messages can be transcribed and shown correctly.

---

### TC-963: Boundary: long voice message

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify transcription works for a long voice message within supported limits.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A long voice message exists in a supported language with acceptable audio quality
- Long message length is within supported limit

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the transcription option for the long voice message | Transcription request is initiated |
| 2 | 2. Wait for transcription to complete | Readable transcription is generated and displayed without truncation |

**Final Expected Result:** Long voice messages within limits are transcribed fully and displayed.

---

### TC-964: User cancels transcription request

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify behavior when user cancels a transcription request in progress.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists in a supported language with acceptable audio quality
- Transcription in-progress indicator is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Initiate transcription for the voice message | Transcription begins and progress indicator appears |
| 2 | 2. Cancel the transcription request | Transcription request is stopped |
| 3 | 3. Observe the message UI | No transcription text is displayed and user can retry |

**Final Expected Result:** Canceled transcriptions stop processing and do not display results.

---

### TC-965: Retry transcription after failure

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-109
**Requirement:** WA-ACC-004

**Description:** Verify user can retry transcription after an unsupported language or poor quality notification.

**Preconditions:**
- User is logged in
- A voice message exists that previously failed transcription due to unsupported language or poor audio quality

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Select the transcription option for the voice message | The system re-evaluates transcription feasibility |
| 2 | 2. Observe the system response | User is informed of the same limitation or transcription proceeds if conditions are now met |

**Final Expected Result:** Retry is supported and the user receives the appropriate outcome based on current conditions.

---

### TC-966: Launch app offline with prior login uses cached data

**Type:** e2e
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify app launches offline and provides access to basic functions using cached data

**Preconditions:**
- User has successfully logged in at least once
- Cached data for basic functions exists on device
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Launch the application | Application opens without error |
| 3 | 3. Navigate to a basic function (e.g., view assigned tasks) | Basic function is accessible and displays cached data |

**Final Expected Result:** Application launches offline and allows access to basic functions using cached data

---

### TC-967: Open app offline without prior login

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify app behavior when no prior login exists and device is offline

**Preconditions:**
- User has never logged in on the device
- No cached data exists
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Launch the application | Application opens to login screen or an offline access restriction screen |
| 3 | 3. Attempt to access a basic function | Access is denied with a clear message indicating login is required |

**Final Expected Result:** App does not allow access without prior login and provides a clear message

---

### TC-968: Save basic function data locally while offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify data saved offline is stored locally and marked pending synchronization

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode
- User is on a basic function screen that supports offline save

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Create or edit data in a basic function form | Form data is entered successfully |
| 3 | 3. Tap Save | System confirms local save and indicates pending synchronization |
| 4 | 4. Open the local pending sync queue or status indicator | Saved record appears with status 'Pending Sync' |

**Final Expected Result:** Data is stored on device and marked as pending synchronization

---

### TC-969: Attempt live-connectivity function while offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify offline restriction message appears and app does not crash

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Navigate to a function that requires live connectivity (e.g., real-time sync or remote search) | Function screen loads or is selectable |
| 3 | 3. Submit the action | System displays a clear offline-unavailable message |

**Final Expected Result:** Action is blocked with a clear message and the app remains stable

---

### TC-970: Recover cached data after app relaunch offline

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify cached data persists across app close/reopen while offline

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Cached data exists on device
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Launch app and view a cached basic function list | Cached data is visible |
| 3 | 3. Close the app completely | App is terminated |
| 4 | 4. Relaunch the app | App opens normally |
| 5 | 5. Revisit the same basic function list | Cached data is still visible |

**Final Expected Result:** Cached data remains available after relaunch while offline

---

### TC-971: Boundary: Save maximum allowed offline data size

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify system can store the maximum supported offline payload and mark pending sync

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode
- Maximum data limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Create a record with maximum allowed data size | Form accepts the maximum size input |
| 3 | 3. Tap Save | System saves data locally without error and marks it pending sync |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum-sized data is saved locally and marked pending synchronization

---

### TC-972: Boundary: Exceed offline data limit

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify system handles oversized offline data gracefully

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode
- Maximum data limits are defined

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Create a record exceeding maximum allowed data size | Input exceeds limits |
| 3 | 3. Tap Save | System blocks save and shows a clear validation message |

**Final Expected Result:** Oversized data is not saved and user receives a clear error message

---

### TC-973: Attempt offline save when local storage is full

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify app handles local storage full condition without crashing

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device storage is full or below required threshold
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Attempt to save data in a basic function | System attempts local save |
| 3 | 3. Observe response | System displays storage error message and does not crash |

**Final Expected Result:** App handles storage full condition gracefully with clear messaging

---

### TC-974: Offline mode indicator is visible

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify user can clearly see that app is in offline mode

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Launch the application | App opens normally |
| 3 | 3. Check for offline indicator or banner | Offline status is clearly displayed |

**Final Expected Result:** User can see a clear offline indicator

---

### TC-975: Attempt live-connectivity function after partial offline navigation

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-110
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-001

**Description:** Verify that after using offline features, live-only actions are still blocked

**Preconditions:**
- User has previously logged in
- Device is set to offline mode

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Ensure device is offline | Network connectivity is unavailable |
| 2 | 2. Use a basic function with cached data | Function works with cached data |
| 3 | 3. Attempt a live-only action from the same workflow | System blocks the action and shows offline-unavailable message |

**Final Expected Result:** Live-only actions remain blocked even after offline navigation

---

### TC-976: Cold start reaches home screen within 2 seconds on typical device

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Validate cold start performance meets 2s requirement on supported device

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- User is logged out or logged in (either state acceptable)
- App is not running (not in background or recent apps)
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec
- Network conditions are normal

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Force close the app and clear it from recent apps | App process is not running |
| 2 | 2. Start a timer and tap the app icon | App begins launching from cold start |
| 3 | 3. Observe the first appearance of the home screen | Home screen is displayed within 2 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Home screen is displayed within 2 seconds on a typical supported device

---

### TC-977: Warm start (background to foreground) resumes last active screen within 1 second

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Verify backgrounded app resumes last active screen within 1 second

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- User is logged in
- App is currently open on a non-home screen
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a non-home screen (e.g., Profile or Settings) | Non-home screen is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Send the app to background (Home button/gesture) | App moves to background |
| 3 | 3. Start a timer and bring the app to foreground from app switcher | App resumes |
| 4 | 4. Observe the displayed screen | Last active screen is shown within 1 second |

**Final Expected Result:** Last active screen is available within 1 second after foregrounding

---

### TC-978: Cold start under limited memory reaches home screen within 3 seconds without crash

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Validate launch performance and stability under low-memory conditions

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- Device has limited available memory (simulated by running multiple apps or memory stress tool)
- App is not running
- Device is a supported device

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simulate limited available memory by opening multiple memory-intensive apps | Device shows low available memory condition |
| 2 | 2. Force close the app and clear it from recent apps | App process is not running |
| 3 | 3. Start a timer and tap the app icon | App begins launching from cold start |
| 4 | 4. Observe the home screen and app stability | Home screen is displayed within 3 seconds and app does not crash |

**Final Expected Result:** App reaches home screen within 3 seconds without crashing under limited memory

---

### TC-979: Boundary test: cold start at exactly 2.0 seconds

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Verify acceptance when launch time equals the threshold

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- App is not running
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec
- Measurement tool available to capture precise timing

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Force close the app and clear it from recent apps | App process is not running |
| 2 | 2. Start a precise timer and tap the app icon | App begins launching |
| 3 | 3. Capture the time when home screen is fully rendered | Measured time is equal to or less than 2.0 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Cold start time at boundary (2.0s) is accepted

---

### TC-980: Negative test: cold start exceeds 2 seconds on typical device

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Ensure failure is detected when cold start exceeds the required threshold

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- App is not running
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec
- Measurement tool available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Force close the app and clear it from recent apps | App process is not running |
| 2 | 2. Start a timer and tap the app icon | App begins launching |
| 3 | 3. Record time when home screen is displayed | Measured time is greater than 2 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Test fails and is logged when launch time exceeds 2 seconds

---

### TC-981: Boundary test: background-to-foreground at exactly 1.0 seconds

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Verify acceptance at the 1 second threshold for resume time

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed and logged in
- App is open on last active screen
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send the app to background | App is in background |
| 2 | 2. Start a precise timer and bring the app to foreground | App begins resuming |
| 3 | 3. Capture the time when last active screen is fully displayed | Measured time is equal to or less than 1.0 second |

**Final Expected Result:** Resume time at boundary (1.0s) is accepted

---

### TC-982: Negative test: background-to-foreground exceeds 1 second

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Ensure failure is detected when resume time exceeds 1 second

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed and logged in
- App is open on last active screen
- Device meets 'typical supported device' spec

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Send the app to background | App is in background |
| 2 | 2. Start a timer and bring the app to foreground | App begins resuming |
| 3 | 3. Record the time when last active screen appears | Measured time is greater than 1 second |

**Final Expected Result:** Test fails and is logged when resume time exceeds 1 second

---

### TC-983: Negative test: low memory launch results in crash

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Verify that a crash is detected if it occurs under low memory

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- Device has limited available memory
- App is not running

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Simulate limited memory by running multiple apps | Device is under memory pressure |
| 2 | 2. Launch the app from a cold start | App attempts to start |
| 3 | 3. Observe app behavior during launch | If app crashes, crash is detected and logged |

**Final Expected Result:** Any crash under low memory is detected and reported; requirement would be considered failed

---

### TC-984: Functional test: last active screen state is preserved on resume

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Ensure correct screen is restored when app returns to foreground

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed and logged in
- App is open and user navigated to a specific screen

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Navigate to a specific screen with unique content (e.g., a specific item details page) | Selected details page is displayed |
| 2 | 2. Send the app to background | App is in background |
| 3 | 3. Bring the app to foreground | App resumes to the same details page |

**Final Expected Result:** Last active screen and its content are preserved on resume

---

### TC-985: Cold start on older supported device meets 2 second requirement

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-111
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-002

**Description:** Validate performance on lower-end supported device within typical spec

**Preconditions:**
- App is installed
- App is not running
- Device is the lowest-end supported device

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Force close the app and clear it from recent apps | App process is not running |
| 2 | 2. Start a timer and tap the app icon | App begins launching |
| 3 | 3. Observe the time to home screen | Home screen is displayed within 2 seconds |

**Final Expected Result:** Cold start meets 2 second target on lowest-end supported device

---

### TC-986: Sync transfers only new/changed messages within performance threshold

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify that only new or modified messages are synchronized and cycle completes within SLA

**Preconditions:**
- Source system contains a mix of unchanged, new, and changed messages
- Target system has baseline data for unchanged messages
- Connection between systems is available
- Performance threshold is configured (e.g., 2 seconds per 100 messages)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start a synchronization cycle | Synchronization begins and connection is established |
| 2 | 2. Monitor transferred messages and timestamps | Only messages marked as new or changed are selected for transfer |
| 3 | 3. Measure total cycle execution time | Cycle completes within the defined performance threshold |
| 4 | 4. Verify target system state after completion | Target has updated data for new/changed messages and unchanged messages are untouched |

**Final Expected Result:** Only new/changed messages are transferred and performance SLA is met

---

### TC-987: No new messages results in no transfers

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify synchronization handles no-op scenario without unnecessary transfers

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has no new or changed messages since last sync
- Connection between systems is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Execute a synchronization cycle | Synchronization starts successfully |
| 2 | 2. Check transfer logs or counts | No messages are transferred |
| 3 | 3. Verify cycle completion time | Cycle completes quickly within normal threshold |

**Final Expected Result:** System performs a no-op sync with zero transfers

---

### TC-988: Large batch processed in configured batches without exceeding resource limits

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Validate batch processing and resource utilization under large load

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has a large backlog of messages (e.g., 100,000)
- Batch size configuration is set (e.g., 1,000 messages per batch)
- Resource limits are configured (CPU, memory, network)

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization for the large backlog | Sync process starts and batch processing is initialized |
| 2 | 2. Observe batch execution | Messages are processed in batches as per configured batch size |
| 3 | 3. Monitor resource usage during sync | CPU, memory, and network usage stay within configured limits |
| 4 | 4. Allow synchronization to complete | All messages are eventually synchronized |

**Final Expected Result:** Large backlog is processed in batches within resource limits and completes successfully

---

### TC-989: Boundary: batch size of 1 processes messages correctly

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Ensure batch processing works at minimum batch size boundary

**Preconditions:**
- Batch size is configured to 1
- Source system has at least 5 pending messages
- Connection between systems is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization | Sync begins with batch size of 1 |
| 2 | 2. Observe message processing | Messages are processed one at a time in separate batches |
| 3 | 3. Verify completion | All pending messages are synchronized |

**Final Expected Result:** System processes single-message batches correctly

---

### TC-990: Boundary: maximum configured batch size handles processing

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Validate behavior at maximum batch size setting

**Preconditions:**
- Batch size is configured to maximum allowed value
- Source system has at least one full batch of messages
- Connection between systems is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Run synchronization | Sync starts with maximum batch size |
| 2 | 2. Observe batch processing and resource usage | Batch processes without exceeding resource limits |
| 3 | 3. Verify batch completion | Batch completes and messages are synchronized |

**Final Expected Result:** Maximum batch size is handled without errors or resource limit violations

---

### TC-991: Network interruption triggers retry and prevents duplicates

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify retry policy behavior and idempotency on connection failure

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has pending messages
- Retry policy is configured (e.g., 3 retries with exponential backoff)
- Connection between systems is initially available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization cycle | Sync begins and initial transfer starts |
| 2 | 2. Simulate network interruption during transfer | System detects connection failure |
| 3 | 3. Observe retry attempts according to policy | Retries occur per configured count and backoff |
| 4 | 4. Restore network connectivity | Synchronization resumes |
| 5 | 5. Verify target system for duplicates | No duplicate messages are created; each message appears once |

**Final Expected Result:** Retries follow policy and no duplicates are produced

---

### TC-992: Retry policy exhaustion results in failure state without duplicates

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Ensure system fails gracefully after retry limit is reached

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has pending messages
- Retry policy is configured to a limited count (e.g., 2 retries)
- Network will remain unavailable during sync

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization cycle | Sync begins and transfer attempts start |
| 2 | 2. Keep network unavailable during all retry attempts | System performs retries and then stops after limit |
| 3 | 3. Check synchronization status | Sync is marked as failed or deferred per design |
| 4 | 4. Validate target system data | No duplicate or partial duplicate records are created |

**Final Expected Result:** System respects retry limit and maintains data integrity

---

### TC-993: Connection unavailable at start prevents synchronization

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Negative test to verify behavior when connection is down before sync begins

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has new messages
- Connection between systems is unavailable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Trigger synchronization cycle | System attempts to establish connection |
| 2 | 2. Observe system response | Sync does not proceed and logs connection failure |
| 3 | 3. Verify no data changes on target | No messages are transferred |

**Final Expected Result:** Synchronization is blocked when connection is unavailable

---

### TC-994: Change detection excludes unchanged messages

**Type:** functional
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify that unchanged messages are not re-synchronized

**Preconditions:**
- Source and target systems are synchronized
- No changes are made to existing messages
- Connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Run synchronization cycle | Sync starts normally |
| 2 | 2. Inspect transferred message list | No unchanged messages are selected for transfer |
| 3 | 3. Validate target system content | Target data remains unchanged |

**Final Expected Result:** Unchanged messages are not transferred

---

### TC-995: Partial batch failure resumes without duplicates

**Type:** integration
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify that a failure within a batch does not create duplicates on retry

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has multiple batches of messages
- Retry policy is configured
- Connection is available initially

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization with multiple batches | First batch begins processing |
| 2 | 2. Inject failure after half the batch is transferred | System detects failure and stops current attempt |
| 3 | 3. Allow retry to occur | System retries the failed batch according to policy |
| 4 | 4. Verify target system data | Messages transferred before failure are not duplicated after retry |

**Final Expected Result:** Batch retry is idempotent and no duplicates are created

---

### TC-996: Performance under sustained synchronization cycles

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** medium
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Verify throughput and latency across consecutive sync cycles

**Preconditions:**
- Source system receives continuous new messages
- Performance threshold is configured
- Connection is stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Execute multiple sync cycles in sequence | Each cycle starts and completes without errors |
| 2 | 2. Measure cycle times across runs | Cycle times remain within threshold across cycles |
| 3 | 3. Verify message delivery timeliness | New messages appear in target system within acceptable delay |

**Final Expected Result:** System sustains performance and timely delivery over consecutive cycles

---

### TC-997: Resource limit breach triggers throttling or safe handling

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** low
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-112
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-003

**Description:** Negative test for configured resource limits under high load

**Preconditions:**
- Source system has high volume of messages
- Resource limits are configured to low thresholds for testing
- Connection is available

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Start synchronization with high volume | Sync begins and processing starts |
| 2 | 2. Observe resource utilization | System throttles or pauses processing when limits are approached |
| 3 | 3. Verify system stability | No crash occurs and sync continues or pauses safely |

**Final Expected Result:** System respects resource limits and handles overuse safely

---

### TC-998: Foreground 30-minute typical usage stays within battery efficiency threshold

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-113
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-004

**Description:** Verify battery consumption during standard foreground usage for 30 minutes meets defined threshold

**Preconditions:**
- App installed on test device
- Battery usage monitoring tool configured
- Device battery at 80% or higher
- Network connectivity stable

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Launch the app and ensure it is in the foreground | App is open and active in the foreground |
| 2 | 2. Perform standard user tasks continuously for 30 minutes (e.g., navigate screens, view content, search, and interact) | All tasks complete successfully without crashes or major delays |
| 3 | 3. Record battery consumption attributed to the app after 30 minutes | Battery usage data for the app is recorded |

**Final Expected Result:** App battery consumption remains within the defined foreground efficiency threshold for 30 minutes of typical usage

---

### TC-999: Background 1-hour idle usage stays within background battery usage limit

**Type:** performance
**Priority:** high
**Status:** manual
**User Story:** US-113
**Requirement:** WA-PERF-004

**Description:** Verify app minimizes background activity and battery drain when device is idle for 1 hour

**Preconditions:**
- App installed and configured
- Battery usage monitoring tool configured
- Device battery at 80% or higher
- Background app refresh enabled per platform settings

**Steps:**

| # | Action | Expected Result |
|---|--------|------------------|
| 1 | 1. Launch the app, then move it to background | App is running in the background |
| 2 | 2. Keep the device idle for 1 hour without user interaction | Device remains idle and app stays in background |
| 3 | 3. Record battery consumption attributed to the app during the 1-hour idle period | Battery usage data for background period is recorded |

**Final Expected Result:** App battery usage in background remains within the defined background usage limit and shows minimized background activity

---
